image | align = right | total_width = 260 | image1 = 浦西境福寿宫中的明教文佛.jpg | width1 = 300 | height1 = 350 | caption1 = 明教文佛 | image2 = 浦西境福寿宫中的度师真君.jpg | width2 = 300 | height2 = 350 | caption2 = 度师真君 | caption_align = center }} 在最早的研究中，以[[李林洲]]为代表的学者通过田野调查，以明教与摩尼教等同、且摩尼教寺院草庵中有带有“文佛”楹联等为理据，认为福寿宫中的明教文佛就是摩尼教的主神摩尼佛，而度师真君则应为某个摩尼师；而这一说法由于证据不足在2004年时遭到了以[[林悟殊]]为代表的摩尼教研究学者的反对。林悟殊认为，明教文佛可能应当依照名称解读为“善于教诲、主管文事”的神明；也不能“勉强把度师真人推测为摩尼师”，而应与福寿宫的建造者萧氏宗族有关。 2008年至2009年间，随着霞浦摩尼教文献的发现，对明教文佛祖殿的两尊主神也有了更明晰的认识{{Cite news|title=福州福寿宫与明教关系密切|author=温海龙|work=福州日报|date=2009-11-17|language=zh}}。明教文佛身上的花纹、配饰，以及身后壁画中的许多画面均与霞浦摩尼教艺术品相类似，尤其是文佛头上的太阳花，福州其他的道教寺庙中没有相类似的装饰，而霞浦摩尼教雕塑中有类似的内容，从而证明了明教文佛确实从属于霞浦摩尼教体系。目前，学界针对明教文佛的身份仍有各种不同的推测，除了大部分观点认同的摩尼教主神摩尼之外，也有观点认为明教文佛是“文佛使者”，或与霞浦文书提到的“[[釋迦文佛]]”“[[泗州大聖|泗州文佛]]”有关。{{Cite journal |author=杨富学 |author2=李晓燕 |author3=彭晓静 |title=福建摩尼教遗存踏查之主要收获 |journal=宗教学研究 |publication-place=[[成都市]] |publication-date=2017 |issue=04 |page=259-271 |issn=1006-1312}} 在霞浦文书的《祷雨疏》《明门初传请本师》等文献中多次出现了“度师”一词，也有以“济南法主四九真人”等称谓指代林瞪，《摩尼施食秘法》更是直接点出“本师教主（林瞪）灵相度师四九真人”；此外，上万村的《济南郡林氏宗谱》、林瞪的八世孙林登鼇则在《八世祖瞪公赞》中均有提到林瞪因在闽县救火有功，邑人在“闽县之右（南方）”为他立庙祭祀。依照闽县境域与方位的推算，明教文佛祖殿原址符合上述文献的描述；另外，宫内度师真人的生辰与林氏宗谱记载的林瞪生辰为同一天，且宫内的壁画、香炉等物件与霞浦发现的摩尼教艺术品风格相似。综合各种因素，度师真人的身份被学界确认为霞浦摩尼教教主林瞪，从而说明浦西境福寿宫是霞浦摩尼教体系的一部分。{{Cite journal |author=杨富学 |title=林瞪及其在中国摩尼教史上的地位 |journal=中国史研究 |language=zh |publication-place=北京市 |publication-date=2014 |issue=01 |page=109-124 |issn=1002-7963}} === 主殿配祀 === {{Multiple image | image1 = 浦西福寿宫张圣真君.jpg | caption1 = 张圣真君 | image2 = 浦西福寿宫玄天上帝.jpg | caption2 = 玄天上帝 | perrow = 2/2 | align = right | total_width = 300 }} 祖殿分别在北壁（主尊所在的方位）西侧和东侧配祀[[张圣真君]]和[[玄天上帝]]，均系道教相关人物。 张圣真君（或称张真君、张圣君）是三十三天监雷法主，出生于[[永泰县|永泰]]，[[閭山派]]祖师[[许真君]]的数传弟子。据上万村村民相传，林瞪曾在闾山修习过许真君所传道法，霞浦文书中也有文献提到闾山派有关的信息，张圣真君以监雷法主的身份配祀的另一个原因则可能是林瞪因祈雨之功被封为“洞天都雷使”{{NoteTag|《兴福祖庆诞科》作“兴福都雷使”，《乐山堂神记》作“感应兴福雷使真君”，《明门初传请本师》作“洞天兴福雷使真君”。}}，与张圣真君的“监雷”身份相关。福州市境内现存有[[张圣真君祖殿]]{{Cite web|date=2023-06-27|title=自然地理|url=http://www.fzmq.gov.cn/xjwz/zjmq/zrdl/201704/t20170413_391146.htm|access-date=2023-10-30|website=闽清县人民政府|archive-date=2022-11-20|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221120015517/http://www.fzmq.gov.cn/xjwz/zjmq/zrdl/201704/t20170413_391146.htm|dead-url=no}}{{Cite journal |author=俞黎媛 |title=传统神灵信仰在当代的变迁与适应——以福建闽清金沙堂张圣君信仰为例 |journal=世界宗教文化 |publication-place=北京 |issue=02 |page=78-81 |issn=1007-6255}}（位于[[闽清县]]）和福建省文物保护单位[[张真君祖殿]]（位于[[台江区]]）{{cite web|publisher= |title=《福建省人民政府关于公布第七批省级文物保护单位名单及保护范围的通知》（闽政文〔2009〕375号） |url=http://www.fujian.gov.cn/zwgk/zxwj/szfwj/200912/t20091209_177441.htm |author=福建省人民政府 |date=2009-11-16 |accessdate=2012-11-12 |deadurl=yes |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131224122739/http://www.fujian.gov.cn/zwgk/zxwj/szfwj/200912/t20091209_177441.htm |archivedate=2013-12-24 }}。 玄天上帝，在霞浦文书中被称作“北方镇天真武菩萨”{{NoteTag|词源自明成祖所加封的“北极镇天真武玄天上帝”}}或“真武大圣”，为[[玄武派]]道教人物，是太上老君第八十二次变化之身，是道教四大护法神（另外三个分别是青龙、白虎和朱雀）之一。在一般的道教寺院中，闾山派和玄武派的人物不能并行出现，而福寿宫则同时供奉了来自两个派系的人物，体现出摩尼教寺院依托道教但又有别于道教的一面。 === 三殿一阁 === 福寿宫拥有四个偏殿，左侧为[[临水夫人]]殿，右侧有[[观音]]阁、[[华光大帝|华光]]殿和[[黄守才|大王]]殿。依据宫内留存的旧石碑的记载，福寿宫的原址旁建有毓麟宫（供奉临水夫人）和大王殿，而观音阁、华光殿为后续兴建。这些偏殿的神明分别属于[[佛教]]、道教系统。 == 注释 == {{NoteFoot}} == 参见 == {{Commons category}} * [[乐山堂]] * [[塔后三佛塔]] * [[飞路塔]] * [[草庵]] == 参考文献 == {{Reflist}} {{摩尼教页脚}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Pu浦}} [[Category:臺江區建築物]] [[Category:福州道教建筑物]] [[Category:摩尼教建筑物]] [[Category:北宋宗教建筑]] [[Category:祖殿]]

revision.comment: 根据参数的变更调整信息框内容
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | building_name = 浦西境福寿宫
（明教文佛祖殿） | image = 浦西福寿宫外景 03.jpg | caption = 浦西境福寿宫外景 | coordinates = {{coord|26|03|46.9|N|119|17|23.21|E|scale:10000|display=title,inline}} | mapframe-zoom = 13 | religious_affiliation = [[明教]]（[[摩尼教]]）
[[道教]]、[[中国民间信仰|福建民间信仰]] | heritage = [[台江区]]不可移动文物点 | architecture_type = [[宫庙]] | totalarea = 约900㎡ | location = {{PRC}}[[福建省]][[福州市]][[台江区]][[义洲街道]]浦西村6-1号 }} '''福寿宫'''{{Cite journal |author=林悟殊 |title=福州浦西福寿宫“明教文佛”宗教属性辨析 |journal=中山大学学报（社会科学版） |publication-date=2004 |issue=06 |page=118-123 |issn=1000-9639}}，又称'''浦西境福寿宫'''{{Cite journal |author=彭晓静 |author2=杨富学 |title=福州福寿宫摩尼教神祇探原 |journal=世界宗教研究 |publication-date=2016 |issue=03 |page=118-128 |issn=1000-4289}}、'''福州福寿宫'''{{Cite journal |author=林子周 |title=福建霞浦与福州福寿宫摩尼教文物比较研究 |journal=[[石河子大学]]学报（哲学社会科学版） |publication-place=[[石河子市]] |publication-date=2016 |issue=06 |page=33-39 |doi=10.13880/j.cnki.cn65-1210/c.20161221.010 |issn=1671-0304}}，旧称'''明教文佛祖殿'''，位于福建省福州市台江区义洲街道浦西社区，是福州市境内仅存的[[摩尼教]]建筑，有“摩尼教文化的活化石”之称，现为福州市台江区不可移动文物点。{{Cite news|url=http://kejiao.cntv.cn/20120509/100101.shtml|title=台江浦西福寿宫被确认为摩尼教寺院遗址|work=[[中国网络电视台]]|date=2012-05-09|accessdate=2023-10-01|language=zh|archive-date=2021-10-21|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211021213535/http://kejiao.cntv.cn/20120509/100101.shtml|dead-url=no}} == 历史 == [[File:浦西福寿宫内殿.jpg|thumb|浦西福寿宫内殿]] 明教文佛祖殿始建于宋代，历年屡经修复与搬迁。依照[[万历]]版[[福宁州]]志中的记载，明教文佛祖殿是为了祭祀北宋[[嘉祐]]年间在[[闽县]]祈雨的摩尼教祖师[[林瞪]]而修建的{{Cite news|title=揭秘“霞浦摩尼教”重大发现|author=茹捷|work=闽东日报|date=2010-04-29|language=zh}}。由于中国历史上（尤其是[[明朝]]时期）对明教大多持禁止态度，当地的萧姓氏族信徒表面上改信[[道教]]，供奉了其他道教、民间信仰神以暗中保护明教文佛的神位{{Cite journal |author=李林洲 |title=福州摩尼教重要遗址——福州台江义洲浦西福寿宫 |journal=福建宗教 |publication-date=2004 |issue=01 |page=43-44}}。1997年及之前，祖殿的原址位于今福州市味精厂，1997年因原址建设味精厂而迁至浦西现址{{Cite journal |author=陈一舟 |author2=涂元济 |title=福建摩尼教寺院遗址考 |journal=海交史研究 |publication-place=[[泉州市]] |publication-date=2004 |issue=01 |page=75-83 |issn=1006-8384}}。由于当时宗教属性不明，1998年，有关部门为令文佛祖殿拥有合法的宗教身份而将其改名为“福寿宫”，并纳入福州的[[道教]]庙宇体系{{Cite book|title=福州市志（1995—2005）|last=福州市地方志编纂委员会|publisher=方志出版社|year=2017|isbn=978-7-5034-9649-3|location=北京|pages=826}}；在2000年代初，由于证据不足，学界仍对福寿宫的宗教属性有极大的争议，直至2008年后[[霞浦文书]]的发现与研究，方确定了福寿宫的宗教属性确为[[摩尼教]]（[[明教]]），且为现今保存最好且配置最完备的摩尼教寺庙。 明教文佛祖殿有一个分殿，位于闽侯县[[上街镇]]石沙自然村，该庙为当地信徒的祖先于200多年前自祖殿分炉而来，庙内祭祀的两个主神明与祖殿相同。 == 建筑 == 明教文佛祖殿1997年及以前的原址坐东朝西，与摩尼教敬西的观念相符，占地3.5亩，建筑面积2亩，包括大殿、配殿、戏台、酒廊看台、厨房和花园等建筑。 浦西境福寿宫的现址总面积900多平方米，坐北朝南。主建筑占地742.6平方米，进深22.6米，大殿宽14.7米，高10米，主要用于祭祀正殿内主供的两尊神：以[[摩尼]]为原型的明教文佛和以福建明教祖师林瞪为原型的度师真君；主殿内主尊左右二侧分别配祀[[张真君]]和[[真武大帝]]，另有三十六护法神将。此外，福寿宫有三座偏殿，分别是临水夫人殿、华光殿和大王殿，供奉[[臨水夫人]]、[[华光大帝]]和黄、赵二大王。 == 神祇系统 == 福寿宫内供奉的各路神祇集摩尼教、[[道教]]、[[佛教]]和当地[[中国民间信仰|民间信仰]]于一宫，也是迄今唯一一座配置完善且香火延续至今的摩尼教寺院。 === 主供神 === 福寿宫主供神为明教文佛和度师真人（又称度师真君），分别代表摩尼教的主神[[摩尼]]（216年—270年代）和[[霞浦县|霞浦]]摩尼教教主林瞪（1003年—1059年）。{{multiple image | align = right | total_width = 260 | image1 = 浦西境福寿宫中的明教文佛.jpg | width1 = 300 | height1 = 350 | caption1 = 明教文佛 | image2 = 浦西境福寿宫中的度师真君.jpg | width2 = 300 | height2 = 350 | caption2 = 度师真君 | caption_align = center }} 在最早的研究中，以[[李林洲]]为代表的学者通过田野调查，以明教与摩尼教等同、且摩尼教寺院草庵中有带有“文佛”楹联等为理据，认为福寿宫中的明教文佛就是摩尼教的主神摩尼佛，而度师真君则应为某个摩尼师；而这一说法由于证据不足在2004年时遭到了以[[林悟殊]]为代表的摩尼教研究学者的反对。林悟殊认为，明教文佛可能应当依照名称解读为“善于教诲、主管文事”的神明；也不能“勉强把度师真人推测为摩尼师”，而应与福寿宫的建造者萧氏宗族有关。 2008年至2009年间，随着霞浦摩尼教文献的发现，对明教文佛祖殿的两尊主神也有了更明晰的认识{{Cite news|title=福州福寿宫与明教关系密切|author=温海龙|work=福州日报|date=2009-11-17|language=zh}}。明教文佛身上的花纹、配饰，以及身后壁画中的许多画面均与霞浦摩尼教艺术品相类似，尤其是文佛头上的太阳花，福州其他的道教寺庙中没有相类似的装饰，而霞浦摩尼教雕塑中有类似的内容，从而证明了明教文佛确实从属于霞浦摩尼教体系。目前，学界针对明教文佛的身份仍有各种不同的推测，除了大部分观点认同的摩尼教主神摩尼之外，也有观点认为明教文佛是“文佛使者”，或与霞浦文书提到的“[[釋迦文佛]]”“[[泗州大聖|泗州文佛]]”有关。{{Cite journal |author=杨富学 |author2=李晓燕 |author3=彭晓静 |title=福建摩尼教遗存踏查之主要收获 |journal=宗教学研究 |publication-place=[[成都市]] |publication-date=2017 |issue=04 |page=259-271 |issn=1006-1312}} 在霞浦文书的《祷雨疏》《明门初传请本师》等文献中多次出现了“度师”一词，也有以“济南法主四九真人”等称谓指代林瞪，《摩尼施食秘法》更是直接点出“本师教主（林瞪）灵相度师四九真人”；此外，上万村的《济南郡林氏宗谱》、林瞪的八世孙林登鼇则在《八世祖瞪公赞》中均有提到林瞪因在闽县救火有功，邑人在“闽县之右（南方）”为他立庙祭祀。依照闽县境域与方位的推算，明教文佛祖殿原址符合上述文献的描述；另外，宫内度师真人的生辰与林氏宗谱记载的林瞪生辰为同一天，且宫内的壁画、香炉等物件与霞浦发现的摩尼教艺术品风格相似。综合各种因素，度师真人的身份被学界确认为霞浦摩尼教教主林瞪，从而说明浦西境福寿宫是霞浦摩尼教体系的一部分。{{Cite journal |author=杨富学 |title=林瞪及其在中国摩尼教史上的地位 |journal=中国史研究 |language=zh |publication-place=北京市 |publication-date=2014 |issue=01 |page=109-124 |issn=1002-7963}} === 主殿配祀 === {{Multiple image | image1 = 浦西福寿宫张圣真君.jpg | caption1 = 张圣真君 | image2 = 浦西福寿宫玄天上帝.jpg | caption2 = 玄天上帝 | perrow = 2/2 | align = right | total_width = 300 }} 祖殿分别在北壁（主尊所在的方位）西侧和东侧配祀[[张圣真君]]和[[玄天上帝]]，均系道教相关人物。 张圣真君（或称张真君、张圣君）是三十三天监雷法主，出生于[[永泰县|永泰]]，[[閭山派]]祖师[[许真君]]的数传弟子。据上万村村民相传，林瞪曾在闾山修习过许真君所传道法，霞浦文书中也有文献提到闾山派有关的信息，张圣真君以监雷法主的身份配祀的另一个原因则可能是林瞪因祈雨之功被封为“洞天都雷使”{{NoteTag|《兴福祖庆诞科》作“兴福都雷使”，《乐山堂神记》作“感应兴福雷使真君”，《明门初传请本师》作“洞天兴福雷使真君”。}}，与张圣真君的“监雷”身份相关。福州市境内现存有[[张圣真君祖殿]]{{Cite web|date=2023-06-27|title=自然地理|url=http://www.fzmq.gov.cn/xjwz/zjmq/zrdl/201704/t20170413_391146.htm|access-date=2023-10-30|website=闽清县人民政府|archive-date=2022-11-20|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221120015517/http://www.fzmq.gov.cn/xjwz/zjmq/zrdl/201704/t20170413_391146.htm|dead-url=no}}{{Cite journal |author=俞黎媛 |title=传统神灵信仰在当代的变迁与适应——以福建闽清金沙堂张圣君信仰为例 |journal=世界宗教文化 |publication-place=北京 |issue=02 |page=78-81 |issn=1007-6255}}（位于[[闽清县]]）和福建省文物保护单位[[张真君祖殿]]（位于[[台江区]]）{{cite web|publisher= |title=《福建省人民政府关于公布第七批省级文物保护单位名单及保护范围的通知》（闽政文〔2009〕375号） |url=http://www.fujian.gov.cn/zwgk/zxwj/szfwj/200912/t20091209_177441.htm |author=福建省人民政府 |date=2009-11-16 |accessdate=2012-11-12 |deadurl=yes |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131224122739/http://www.fujian.gov.cn/zwgk/zxwj/szfwj/200912/t20091209_177441.htm |archivedate=2013-12-24 }}。 玄天上帝，在霞浦文书中被称作“北方镇天真武菩萨”{{NoteTag|词源自明成祖所加封的“北极镇天真武玄天上帝”}}或“真武大圣”，为[[玄武派]]道教人物，是太上老君第八十二次变化之身，是道教四大护法神（另外三个分别是青龙、白虎和朱雀）之一。在一般的道教寺院中，闾山派和玄武派的人物不能并行出现，而福寿宫则同时供奉了来自两个派系的人物，体现出摩尼教寺院依托道教但又有别于道教的一面。 === 三殿一阁 === 福寿宫拥有四个偏殿，左侧为[[临水夫人]]殿，右侧有[[观音]]阁、[[华光大帝|华光]]殿和[[黄守才|大王]]殿。依据宫内留存的旧石碑的记载，福寿宫的原址旁建有毓麟宫（供奉临水夫人）和大王殿，而观音阁、华光殿为后续兴建。这些偏殿的神明分别属于[[佛教]]、道教系统。 == 注释 == {{NoteFoot}} == 参见 == {{Commons category}} * [[乐山堂]] * [[塔后三佛塔]] * [[飞路塔]] * [[草庵]] == 参考文献 == {{Reflist}} {{摩尼教页脚}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Pu浦}} [[Category:臺江區建築物]] [[Category:福州道教建筑物]] [[Category:摩尼教建筑物]] [[Category:北宋宗教建筑]] [[Category:祖殿]]

{{Infobox religious building | building_name = 浦西境福寿宫
（明教文佛祖殿） | image = 浦西福寿宫外景 03.jpg | caption = 浦西境福寿宫外景 | coordinates = {{coord|26|03|46.9|N|119|17|23.21|E|scale:10000|display=title,inline}} | mapframe-zoom = 13 | religious_affiliation = [[明教]]（[[摩尼教]]）
[[道教]]、[[中国民间信仰|福建民间信仰]] | heritage = [[台江区]]不可移动文物点 | architecture_type = [[宫庙]] | totalarea = 约900㎡ | location = {{PRC}}[[福建省]][[福州市]][[台江区]][[义洲街道]]浦西村6-1号 }} '''福寿宫'''{{Cite journal |author=林悟殊 |title=福州浦西福寿宫“明教文佛”宗教属性辨析 |journal=中山大学学报（社会科学版） |publication-date=2004 |issue=06 |page=118-123 |issn=1000-9639}}，又称'''浦西境福寿宫'''{{Cite journal |author=彭晓静 |author2=杨富学 |title=福州福寿宫摩尼教神祇探原 |journal=世界宗教研究 |publication-date=2016 |issue=03 |page=118-128 |issn=1000-4289}}、'''福州福寿宫'''{{Cite journal |author=林子周 |title=福建霞浦与福州福寿宫摩尼教文物比较研究 |journal=[[石河子大学]]学报（哲学社会科学版） |publication-place=[[石河子市]] |publication-date=2016 |issue=06 |page=33-39 |doi=10.13880/j.cnki.cn65-1210/c.20161221.010 |issn=1671-0304}}，旧称'''明教文佛祖殿'''，是位于福建省福州市台江区义洲街道浦西社区的一棟宗教建築物，为福州市境内仅存的[[摩尼教]]建筑，有“摩尼教文化的活化石”之称，现为福州市台江区不可移动文物点。{{Cite news|url=http://kejiao.cntv.cn/20120509/100101.shtml|title=台江浦西福寿宫被确认为摩尼教寺院遗址|work=[[中国网络电视台]]|date=2012-05-09|accessdate=2023-10-01|language=zh|archive-date=2021-10-21|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211021213535/http://kejiao.cntv.cn/20120509/100101.shtml|dead-url=no}}浦西福壽宮同時也是[[九案泰山]]信仰中的十三個堂之一。 == 历史 == [[File:浦西福寿宫内殿.jpg|thumb|浦西福寿宫内殿]] 明教文佛祖殿始建于宋代，历年屡经修复与搬迁。依照[[万历]]版[[福宁州]]志中的记载，明教文佛祖殿是为了祭祀北宋[[嘉祐]]年间在[[闽县]]祈雨的摩尼教祖师[[林瞪]]而修建的{{Cite news|title=揭秘“霞浦摩尼教”重大发现|author=茹捷|work=闽东日报|date=2010-04-29|language=zh}}。由于中国历史上（尤其是[[明朝]]时期）对明教大多持禁止态度，当地的萧姓氏族信徒表面上改信[[道教]]，供奉了其他道教、民间信仰神以暗中保护明教文佛的神位{{Cite journal |author=李林洲 |title=福州摩尼教重要遗址——福州台江义洲浦西福寿宫 |journal=福建宗教 |publication-date=2004 |issue=01 |page=43-44}}。1997年及之前，祖殿的原址位于今福州市味精厂，1997年因原址建设味精厂而迁至浦西现址{{Cite journal |author=陈一舟 |author2=涂元济 |title=福建摩尼教寺院遗址考 |journal=海交史研究 |publication-place=[[泉州市]] |publication-date=2004 |issue=01 |page=75-83 |issn=1006-8384}}。由于当时宗教属性不明，1998年，有关部门为令文佛祖殿拥有合法的宗教身份而将其改名为“福寿宫”，并纳入福州的[[道教]]庙宇体系{{Cite book|title=福州市志（1995—2005）|last=福州市地方志编纂委员会|publisher=方志出版社|year=2017|isbn=978-7-5034-9649-3|location=北京|pages=826}}；在2000年代初，由于证据不足，学界仍对福寿宫的宗教属性有极大的争议，直至2008年后[[霞浦文书]]的发现与研究，方确定了福寿宫的宗教属性确为[[摩尼教]]（[[明教]]），且为现今保存最好且配置最完备的摩尼教寺庙。 明教文佛祖殿有一个分殿，位于闽侯县[[上街镇]]石沙自然村，该庙为当地信徒的祖先于200多年前自祖殿分炉而来，庙内祭祀的两个主神明与祖殿相同。 == 建筑 == 明教文佛祖殿1997年及以前的原址坐东朝西，与摩尼教敬西的观念相符，占地3.5亩，建筑面积2亩，包括大殿、配殿、戏台、酒廊看台、厨房和花园等建筑。 浦西境福寿宫的现址总面积900多平方米，坐北朝南。主建筑占地742.6平方米，进深22.6米，大殿宽14.7米，高10米，主要用于祭祀正殿内主供的两尊神：以[[摩尼]]为原型的明教文佛和以福建明教祖师林瞪为原型的度师真君；主殿内主尊左右二侧分别配祀[[张真君]]和[[真武大帝]]，另有三十六护法神将。此外，福寿宫有三座偏殿，分别是临水夫人殿、华光殿和大王殿，供奉[[臨水夫人]]、[[华光大帝]]和黄、赵二大王。 == 神祇系统 == 福寿宫内供奉的各路神祇集摩尼教、[[道教]]、[[佛教]]和当地[[中国民间信仰|民间信仰]]于一宫，也是迄今唯一一座配置完善且香火延续至今的摩尼教寺院。 === 主供神 === 福寿宫主供神为明教文佛和度师真人（又称度师真君），分别代表摩尼教的主神[[摩尼]]（216年—270年代）和[[霞浦县|霞浦]]摩尼教教主林瞪（1003年—1059年）。{{multiple image | align = right | total_width = 260 | image1 = 浦西境福寿宫中的明教文佛.jpg | width1 = 300 | height1 = 350 | caption1 = 明教文佛 | image2 = 浦西境福寿宫中的度师真君.jpg | width2 = 300 | height2 = 350 | caption2 = 度师真君 | caption_align = center }} 在最早的研究中，以[[李林洲]]为代表的学者通过田野调查，以明教与摩尼教等同、且摩尼教寺院草庵中有带有“文佛”楹联等为理据，认为福寿宫中的明教文佛就是摩尼教的主神摩尼佛，而度师真君则应为某个摩尼师；而这一说法由于证据不足在2004年时遭到了以[[林悟殊]]为代表的摩尼教研究学者的反对。林悟殊认为，明教文佛可能应当依照名称解读为“善于教诲、主管文事”的神明；也不能“勉强把度师真人推测为摩尼师”，而应与福寿宫的建造者萧氏宗族有关。 2008年至2009年间，随着霞浦摩尼教文献的发现，对明教文佛祖殿的两尊主神也有了更明晰的认识{{Cite news|title=福州福寿宫与明教关系密切|author=温海龙|work=福州日报|date=2009-11-17|language=zh}}。明教文佛身上的花纹、配饰，以及身后壁画中的许多画面均与霞浦摩尼教艺术品相类似，尤其是文佛头上的太阳花，福州其他的道教寺庙中没有相类似的装饰，而霞浦摩尼教雕塑中有类似的内容，从而证明了明教文佛确实从属于霞浦摩尼教体系。目前，学界针对明教文佛的身份仍有各种不同的推测，除了大部分观点认同的摩尼教主神摩尼之外，也有观点认为明教文佛是“文佛使者”，或与霞浦文书提到的“[[釋迦文佛]]”“[[泗州大聖|泗州文佛]]”有关。{{Cite journal |author=杨富学 |author2=李晓燕 |author3=彭晓静 |title=福建摩尼教遗存踏查之主要收获 |journal=宗教学研究 |publication-place=[[成都市]] |publication-date=2017 |issue=04 |page=259-271 |issn=1006-1312}} 在霞浦文书的《祷雨疏》《明门初传请本师》等文献中多次出现了“度师”一词，也有以“济南法主四九真人”等称谓指代林瞪，《摩尼施食秘法》更是直接点出“本师教主（林瞪）灵相度师四九真人”；此外，上万村的《济南郡林氏宗谱》、林瞪的八世孙林登鼇则在《八世祖瞪公赞》中均有提到林瞪因在闽县救火有功，邑人在“闽县之右（南方）”为他立庙祭祀。依照闽县境域与方位的推算，明教文佛祖殿原址符合上述文献的描述；另外，宫内度师真人的生辰与林氏宗谱记载的林瞪生辰为同一天，且宫内的壁画、香炉等物件与霞浦发现的摩尼教艺术品风格相似。综合各种因素，度师真人的身份被学界确认为霞浦摩尼教教主林瞪，从而说明浦西境福寿宫是霞浦摩尼教体系的一部分。{{Cite journal |author=杨富学 |title=林瞪及其在中国摩尼教史上的地位 |journal=中国史研究 |language=zh |publication-place=北京市 |publication-date=2014 |issue=01 |page=109-124 |issn=1002-7963}} === 主殿配祀 === {{Multiple image | image1 = 浦西福寿宫张圣真君.jpg | caption1 = 张圣真君 | image2 = 浦西福寿宫玄天上帝.jpg | caption2 = 玄天上帝 | perrow = 2/2 | align = right | total_width = 300 }} 祖殿分别在北壁（主尊所在的方位）西侧和东侧配祀[[张圣真君]]和[[玄天上帝]]，均系道教相关人物。 张圣真君（或称张真君、张圣君）是三十三天监雷法主，出生于[[永泰县|永泰]]，[[閭山派]]祖师[[许真君]]的数传弟子。据上万村村民相传，林瞪曾在闾山修习过许真君所传道法，霞浦文书中也有文献提到闾山派有关的信息，张圣真君以监雷法主的身份配祀的另一个原因则可能是林瞪因祈雨之功被封为“洞天都雷使”{{NoteTag|《兴福祖庆诞科》作“兴福都雷使”，《乐山堂神记》作“感应兴福雷使真君”，《明门初传请本师》作“洞天兴福雷使真君”。}}，与张圣真君的“监雷”身份相关。福州市境内现存有[[张圣真君祖殿]]{{Cite web|date=2023-06-27|title=自然地理|url=http://www.fzmq.gov.cn/xjwz/zjmq/zrdl/201704/t20170413_391146.htm|access-date=2023-10-30|website=闽清县人民政府|archive-date=2022-11-20|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221120015517/http://www.fzmq.gov.cn/xjwz/zjmq/zrdl/201704/t20170413_391146.htm|dead-url=no}}{{Cite journal |author=俞黎媛 |title=传统神灵信仰在当代的变迁与适应——以福建闽清金沙堂张圣君信仰为例 |journal=世界宗教文化 |publication-place=北京 |issue=02 |page=78-81 |issn=1007-6255}}（位于[[闽清县]]）和福建省文物保护单位[[张真君祖殿]]（位于[[台江区]]）{{cite web|publisher= |title=《福建省人民政府关于公布第七批省级文物保护单位名单及保护范围的通知》（闽政文〔2009〕375号） |url=http://www.fujian.gov.cn/zwgk/zxwj/szfwj/200912/t20091209_177441.htm |author=福建省人民政府 |date=2009-11-16 |accessdate=2012-11-12 |deadurl=yes |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131224122739/http://www.fujian.gov.cn/zwgk/zxwj/szfwj/200912/t20091209_177441.htm |archivedate=2013-12-24 }}。 玄天上帝，在霞浦文书中被称作“北方镇天真武菩萨”{{NoteTag|词源自明成祖所加封的“北极镇天真武玄天上帝”}}或“真武大圣”，为[[玄武派]]道教人物，是太上老君第八十二次变化之身，是道教四大护法神（另外三个分别是青龙、白虎和朱雀）之一。在一般的道教寺院中，闾山派和玄武派的人物不能并行出现，而福寿宫则同时供奉了来自两个派系的人物，体现出摩尼教寺院依托道教但又有别于道教的一面。 === 三殿一阁 === 福寿宫拥有四个偏殿，左侧为[[临水夫人]]殿，右侧有[[观音]]阁、[[华光大帝|华光]]殿和[[黄守才|大王]]殿。依据宫内留存的旧石碑的记载，福寿宫的原址旁建有毓麟宫（供奉临水夫人）和大王殿，而观音阁、华光殿为后续兴建。这些偏殿的神明分别属于[[佛教]]、道教系统。 == 注释 == {{NoteFoot}} == 参见 == {{Commons category}} * news|url=http://kejiao.cntv.cn/20120509/100101.shtml|title=台江浦西福寿宫被确认为摩尼教寺院遗址|work=[[中国网络电视台]]|date=2012-05-09|accessdate=2023-10-01|language=zh|archive-date=2021-10-21|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211021213535/http://kejiao.cntv.cn/20120509/100101.shtml|dead-url=no}}浦西福壽宮同時也是[[九案泰山]]信仰中的十三個堂之一。 news|title=揭秘“霞浦摩尼教”重大发现|author=茹捷|work=闽东日报|date=2010-04-29|language=zh}}。由于中国历史上（尤其是[[明朝]]时期）对明教大多持禁止态度，当地的萧姓氏族信徒表面上改信[[道教]]，供奉了其他道教、民间信仰神以暗中保护明教文佛的神位{{Cite journal |author=李林洲 |title=福州摩尼教重要遗址——福州台江义洲浦西福寿宫 |journal=福建宗教 |publication-date=2004 |issue=01 |page=43-44}}。1997年及之前，祖殿的原址位于今福州市味精厂，1997年因原址建设味精厂而迁至浦西现址{{Cite journal |author=陈一舟 |author2=涂元济 |title=福建摩尼教寺院遗址考 |journal=海交史研究 |publication-place=[[泉州市]] |publication-date=2004 |issue=01 |page=75-83 |issn=1006-8384}}。由于当时宗教属性不明，1998年，有关部门为令文佛祖殿拥有合法的宗教身份而将其改名为“福寿宫”，并纳入福州的[[道教]]庙宇体系{{Cite book|title=福州市志（1995—2005）|last=福州市地方志编纂委员会|publisher=方志出版社|year=2017|isbn=978-7-5034-9649-3|location=北京|pages=826}}；在2000年代初，由于证据不足，学界仍对福寿宫的宗教属性有极大的争议，直至2008年后[[霞浦文书]]的发现与研究，方确定了福寿宫的宗教属性确为[[摩尼教]]（[[明教]]），且为现今保存最好且配置最完备的摩尼教寺庙。 明教文佛祖殿有一个分殿，位于闽侯县[[上街镇]]石沙自然村，该庙为当地信徒的祖先于200多年前自祖殿分炉而来，庙内祭祀的两个主神明与祖殿相同。 == 建筑 == 明教文佛祖殿1997年及以前的原址坐东朝西，与摩尼教敬西的观念相符，占地3.5亩，建筑面积2亩，包括大殿、配殿、戏台、酒廊看台、厨房和花园等建筑。 浦西境福寿宫的现址总面积900多平方米，坐北朝南。主建筑占地742.6平方米，进深22.6米，大殿宽14.7米，高10米，主要用于祭祀正殿内主供的两尊神：以[[摩尼]]为原型的明教文佛和以福建明教祖师林瞪为原型的度师真君；主殿内主尊左右二侧分别配祀[[张真君]]和[[真武大帝]]，另有三十六护法神将。此外，福寿宫有三座偏殿，分别是临水夫人殿、华光殿和大王殿，供奉[[臨水夫人]]、[[华光大帝]]和黄、赵二大王。 == 神祇系统 == 福寿宫内供奉的各路神祇集摩尼教、[[道教]]、[[佛教]]和当地[[中国民间信仰|民间信仰]]于一宫，也是迄今唯一一座配置完善且香火延续至今的摩尼教寺院。 === 主供神 === 福寿宫主供神为明教文佛和度师真人（又称度师真君），分别代表摩尼教的主神[[摩尼]]（216年—270年代）和[[霞浦县|霞浦]]摩尼教教主林瞪（1003年—1059年）。{{multiple image | align = right | total_width = 260 | image1 = 浦西境福寿宫中的明教文佛.jpg | width1 = 300 | height1 = 350 | caption1 = 明教文佛 | image2 = 浦西境福寿宫中的度师真君.jpg | width2 = 300 | height2 = 350 | caption2 = 度师真君 | caption_align = center }} 在最早的研究中，以[[李林洲]]为代表的学者通过田野调查，以明教与摩尼教等同、且摩尼教寺院草庵中有带有“文佛”楹联等为理据，认为福寿宫中的明教文佛就是摩尼教的主神摩尼佛，而度师真君则应为某个摩尼师；而这一说法由于证据不足在2004年时遭到了以[[林悟殊]]为代表的摩尼教研究学者的反对。林悟殊认为，明教文佛可能应当依照名称解读为“善于教诲、主管文事”的神明；也不能“勉强把度师真人推测为摩尼师”，而应与福寿宫的建造者萧氏宗族有关。 2008年至2009年间，随着霞浦摩尼教文献的发现，对明教文佛祖殿的两尊主神也有了更明晰的认识{{Cite news|title=福州福寿宫与明教关系密切|author=温海龙|work=福州日报|date=2009-11-17|language=zh}}。明教文佛身上的花纹、配饰，以及身后壁画中的许多画面均与霞浦摩尼教艺术品相类似，尤其是文佛头上的太阳花，福州其他的道教寺庙中没有相类似的装饰，而霞浦摩尼教雕塑中有类似的内容，从而证明了明教文佛确实从属于霞浦摩尼教体系。目前，学界针对明教文佛的身份仍有各种不同的推测，除了大部分观点认同的摩尼教主神摩尼之外，也有观点认为明教文佛是“文佛使者”，或与霞浦文书提到的“[[釋迦文佛]]”“[[泗州大聖|泗州文佛]]”有关。{{Cite journal |author=杨富学 |author2=李晓燕 |author3=彭晓静 |title=福建摩尼教遗存踏查之主要收获 |journal=宗教学研究 |publication-place=[[成都市]] |publication-date=2017 |issue=04 |page=259-271 |issn=1006-1312}} 在霞浦文书的《祷雨疏》《明门初传请本师》等文献中多次出现了“度师”一词，也有以“济南法主四九真人”等称谓指代林瞪，《摩尼施食秘法》更是直接点出“本师教主（林瞪）灵相度师四九真人”；此外，上万村的《济南郡林氏宗谱》、林瞪的八世孙林登鼇则在《八世祖瞪公赞》中均有提到林瞪因在闽县救火有功，邑人在“闽县之右（南方）”为他立庙祭祀。依照闽县境域与方位的推算，明教文佛祖殿原址符合上述文献的描述；另外，宫内度师真人的生辰与林氏宗谱记载的林瞪生辰为同一天，且宫内的壁画、香炉等物件与霞浦发现的摩尼教艺术品风格相似。综合各种因素，度师真人的身份被学界确认为霞浦摩尼教教主林瞪，从而说明浦西境福寿宫是霞浦摩尼教体系的一部分。{{Cite journal |author=杨富学 |title=林瞪及其在中国摩尼教史上的地位 |journal=中国史研究 |language=zh |publication-place=北京市 |publication-date=2014 |issue=01 |page=109-124 |issn=1002-7963}} === 主殿配祀 === {{Multiple image | image1 = 浦西福寿宫张圣真君.jpg | caption1 = 张圣真君 | image2 = 浦西福寿宫玄天上帝.jpg | caption2 = 玄天上帝 | perrow = 2/2 | align = right | total_width = 300 }} 祖殿分别在北壁（主尊所在的方位）西侧和东侧配祀[[张圣真君]]和[[玄天上帝]]，均系道教相关人物。 张圣真君（或称张真君、张圣君）是三十三天监雷法主，出生于[[永泰县|永泰]]，[[閭山派]]祖师[[许真君]]的数传弟子。据上万村村民相传，林瞪曾在闾山修习过许真君所传道法，霞浦文书中也有文献提到闾山派有关的信息，张圣真君以监雷法主的身份配祀的另一个原因则可能是林瞪因祈雨之功被封为“洞天都雷使”{{NoteTag|《兴福祖庆诞科》作“兴福都雷使”，《乐山堂神记》作“感应兴福雷使真君”，《明门初传请本师》作“洞天兴福雷使真君”。}}，与张圣真君的“监雷”身份相关。福州市境内现存有[[张圣真君祖殿]]{{Cite web|date=2023-06-27|title=自然地理|url=http://www.fzmq.gov.cn/xjwz/zjmq/zrdl/201704/t20170413_391146.htm|access-date=2023-10-30|website=闽清县人民政府|archive-date=2022-11-20|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221120015517/http://www.fzmq.gov.cn/xjwz/zjmq/zrdl/201704/t20170413_391146.htm|dead-url=no}}{{Cite journal |author=俞黎媛 |title=传统神灵信仰在当代的变迁与适应——以福建闽清金沙堂张圣君信仰为例 |journal=世界宗教文化 |publication-place=北京 |issue=02 |page=78-81 |issn=1007-6255}}（位于[[闽清县]]）和福建省文物保护单位[[张真君祖殿]]（位于[[台江区]]）{{cite web|publisher= |title=《福建省人民政府关于公布第七批省级文物保护单位名单及保护范围的通知》（闽政文〔2009〕375号） |url=http://www.fujian.gov.cn/zwgk/zxwj/szfwj/200912/t20091209_177441.htm |author=福建省人民政府 |date=2009-11-16 |accessdate=2012-11-12 |deadurl=yes |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131224122739/http://www.fujian.gov.cn/zwgk/zxwj/szfwj/200912/t20091209_177441.htm |archivedate=2013-12-24 }}。 玄天上帝，在霞浦文书中被称作“北方镇天真武菩萨”{{NoteTag|词源自明成祖所加封的“北极镇天真武玄天上帝”}}或“真武大圣”，为[[玄武派]]道教人物，是太上老君第八十二次变化之身，是道教四大护法神（另外三个分别是青龙、白虎和朱雀）之一。在一般的道教寺院中，闾山派和玄武派的人物不能并行出现，而福寿宫则同时供奉了来自两个派系的人物，体现出摩尼教寺院依托道教但又有别于道教的一面。 === 三殿一阁 === 福寿宫拥有四个偏殿，左侧为[[临水夫人]]殿，右侧有[[观音]]阁、[[华光大帝|华光]]殿和[[黄守才|大王]]殿。依据宫内留存的旧石碑的记载，福寿宫的原址旁建有毓麟宫（供奉临水夫人）和大王殿，而观音阁、华光殿为后续兴建。这些偏殿的神明分别属于[[佛教]]、道教系统。 == 注释 == {{NoteFoot}} == 参见 == {{Commons category}} {{fzcuo|1742|浦西福寿宫}} * [[乐山堂]] * [[塔后三佛塔]] * [[飞路塔]] * [[草庵]] == 参考文献 == {{Reflist}} {{摩尼教页脚}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Pu浦}} [[Category:臺江區建築物]] [[Category:福州道教建筑物]] [[Category:摩尼教建筑物]] [[Category:北宋宗教建筑]] [[Category:祖殿]]

{{Infobox religious building | building_name = 浦西境福寿宫
（明教文佛祖殿） | image = 浦西福寿宫外景 03.jpg | caption = 浦西境福寿宫外景 | coordinates = {{coord|26|3|46.76|N|119|17|23.82|E|scale:10000|display=title,inline}} | mapframe-zoom = 13 | religious_affiliation = [[明教]]（[[摩尼教]]）
[[道教]]、[[中国民间信仰|福建民间信仰]] | heritage = [[台江区]]不可移动文物点 | architecture_type = [[宫庙]] | totalarea = 约900㎡ | location = {{PRC}}[[福建省]][[福州市]][[台江区]][[义洲街道]]浦西村6-1号 }} '''福寿宫'''{{Cite journal |author=林悟殊 |title=福州浦西福寿宫“明教文佛”宗教属性辨析 |journal=中山大学学报（社会科学版） |publication-date=2004 |issue=06 |page=118-123 |issn=1000-9639}}，又称'''浦西境福寿宫'''{{Cite journal |author=彭晓静 |author2=杨富学 |title=福州福寿宫摩尼教神祇探原 |journal=世界宗教研究 |publication-date=2016 |issue=03 |page=118-128 |issn=1000-4289}}、'''福州福寿宫'''{{Cite journal |author=林子周 |title=福建霞浦与福州福寿宫摩尼教文物比较研究 |journal=[[石河子大学]]学报（哲学社会科学版） |publication-place=[[石河子市]] |publication-date=2016 |issue=06 |page=33-39 |doi=10.13880/j.cnki.cn65-1210/c.20161221.010 |issn=1671-0304}}，旧称'''明教文佛祖殿'''，是位于福建省福州市台江区义洲街道浦西社区的一棟宗教建築物，为福州市境内仅存的[[摩尼教]]建筑，有“摩尼教文化的活化石”之称，现为福州市台江区不可移动文物点。{{Cite news|url=http://kejiao.cntv.cn/20120509/100101.shtml|title=台江浦西福寿宫被确认为摩尼教寺院遗址|work=[[中国网络电视台]]|date=2012-05-09|accessdate=2023-10-01|language=zh|archive-date=2021-10-21|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211021213535/http://kejiao.cntv.cn/20120509/100101.shtml|dead-url=no}}浦西福壽宮同時也是[[九案泰山]]信仰中的十三個堂之一。 == 历史 == [[File:浦西福寿宫内殿.jpg|thumb|浦西福寿宫内殿]] 明教文佛祖殿始建于宋代，历年屡经修复与搬迁。依照[[万历]]版[[福宁州]]志中的记载，明教文佛祖殿是为了祭祀北宋[[嘉祐]]年间在[[闽县]]祈雨的摩尼教祖师[[林瞪]]而修建的{{Cite news|title=揭秘“霞浦摩尼教”重大发现|author=茹捷|work=闽东日报|date=2010-04-29|language=zh}}。由于中国历史上（尤其是[[明朝]]时期）对明教大多持禁止态度，当地的萧姓氏族信徒表面上改信[[道教]]，供奉了其他道教、民间信仰神以暗中保护明教文佛的神位{{Cite journal |author=李林洲 |title=福州摩尼教重要遗址——福州台江义洲浦西福寿宫 |journal=福建宗教 |publication-date=2004 |issue=01 |page=43-44}}。1997年及之前，祖殿的原址位于今福州市味精厂，1997年因原址建设味精厂而迁至浦西现址{{Cite journal |author=陈一舟 |author2=涂元济 |title=福建摩尼教寺院遗址考 |journal=海交史研究 |publication-place=[[泉州市]] |publication-date=2004 |issue=01 |page=75-83 |issn=1006-8384}}。由于当时宗教属性不明，1998年，有关部门为令文佛祖殿拥有合法的宗教身份而将其改名为“福寿宫”，并纳入福州的[[道教]]庙宇体系{{Cite book|title=福州市志（1995—2005）|last=福州市地方志编纂委员会|publisher=方志出版社|year=2017|isbn=978-7-5034-9649-3|location=北京|pages=826}}；在2000年代初，由于证据不足，学界仍对福寿宫的宗教属性有极大的争议，直至2008年后[[霞浦文书]]的发现与研究，方确定了福寿宫的宗教属性确为[[摩尼教]]（[[明教]]），且为现今保存最好且配置最完备的摩尼教寺庙。 明教文佛祖殿有一个分殿，位于闽侯县[[上街镇]]石沙自然村，该庙为当地信徒的祖先于200多年前自祖殿分炉而来，庙内祭祀的两个主神明与祖殿相同。 == 建筑 == 明教文佛祖殿1997年及以前的原址坐东朝西，与摩尼教敬西的观念相符，占地3.5亩，建筑面积2亩，包括大殿、配殿、戏台、酒廊看台、厨房和花园等建筑。 浦西境福寿宫的现址总面积900多平方米，坐北朝南。主建筑占地742.6平方米，进深22.6米，大殿宽14.7米，高10米，主要用于祭祀正殿内主供的两尊神：以[[摩尼]]为原型的明教文佛和以福建明教祖师林瞪为原型的度师真君；主殿内主尊左右二侧分别配祀[[张真君]]和[[真武大帝]]，另有三十六护法神将。此外，福寿宫有三座偏殿，分别是临水夫人殿、华光殿和大王殿，供奉[[臨水夫人]]、[[华光大帝]]和黄、赵二大王。 caption_align = center }} news|title=福州福寿宫与明教关系密切|author=温海龙|work=福州日报|date=2009-11-17|language=zh}}。明教文佛身上的花纹、配饰，以及身后壁画中的许多画面均与霞浦摩尼教艺术品相类似，尤其是文佛头上的太阳花，福州其他的道教寺庙中没有相类似的装饰，而霞浦摩尼教雕塑中有类似的内容，从而证明了明教文佛确实从属于霞浦摩尼教体系。目前，学界针对明教文佛的身份仍有各种不同的推测，除了大部分观点认同的摩尼教主神摩尼之外，也有观点认为明教文佛是“文佛使者”，或与霞浦文书提到的“[[釋迦文佛]]”“[[泗州大聖|泗州文佛]]”有关。{{Cite journal |author=杨富学 |author2=李晓燕 |author3=彭晓静 |title=福建摩尼教遗存踏查之主要收获 |journal=宗教学研究 |publication-place=[[成都市]] |publication-date=2017 |issue=04 |page=259-271 |issn=1006-1312}} 在霞浦文书的《祷雨疏》《明门初传请本师》等文献中多次出现了“度师”一词，也有以“济南法主四九真人”等称谓指代林瞪，《摩尼施食秘法》更是直接点出“本师教主（林瞪）灵相度师四九真人”；此外，上万村的《济南郡林氏宗谱》、林瞪的八世孙林登鼇则在《八世祖瞪公赞》中均有提到林瞪因在闽县救火有功，邑人在“闽县之右（南方）”为他立庙祭祀。依照闽县境域与方位的推算，明教文佛祖殿原址符合上述文献的描述；另外，宫内度师真人的生辰与林氏宗谱记载的林瞪生辰为同一天，且宫内的壁画、香炉等物件与霞浦发现的摩尼教艺术品风格相似。综合各种因素，度师真人的身份被学界确认为霞浦摩尼教教主林瞪，从而说明浦西境福寿宫是霞浦摩尼教体系的一部分。{{Cite journal |author=杨富学 |title=林瞪及其在中国摩尼教史上的地位 |journal=中国史研究 |language=zh |publication-place=北京市 |publication-date=2014 |issue=01 |page=109-124 |issn=1002-7963}} === 主殿配祀 === {{Multiple image | image1 = 浦西福寿宫张圣真君.jpg | caption1 = 张圣真君 | image2 = 浦西福寿宫玄天上帝.jpg | caption2 = 玄天上帝 | perrow = 2/2 | align = right | total_width = 300 }} 祖殿分别在北壁（主尊所在的方位）西侧和东侧配祀[[张圣真君]]和[[玄天上帝]]，均系道教相关人物。 张圣真君（或称张真君、张圣君）是三十三天监雷法主，出生于[[永泰县|永泰]]，[[閭山派]]祖师[[许真君]]的数传弟子。据上万村村民相传，林瞪曾在闾山修习过许真君所传道法，霞浦文书中也有文献提到闾山派有关的信息，张圣真君以监雷法主的身份配祀的另一个原因则可能是林瞪因祈雨之功被封为“洞天都雷使”{{NoteTag|《兴福祖庆诞科》作“兴福都雷使”，《乐山堂神记》作“感应兴福雷使真君”，《明门初传请本师》作“洞天兴福雷使真君”。}}，与张圣真君的“监雷”身份相关。福州市境内现存有[[张圣真君祖殿]]{{Cite web|date=2023-06-27|title=自然地理|url=http://www.fzmq.gov.cn/xjwz/zjmq/zrdl/201704/t20170413_391146.htm|access-date=2023-10-30|website=闽清县人民政府|archive-date=2022-11-20|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221120015517/http://www.fzmq.gov.cn/xjwz/zjmq/zrdl/201704/t20170413_391146.htm|dead-url=no}}{{Cite journal |author=俞黎媛 |title=传统神灵信仰在当代的变迁与适应——以福建闽清金沙堂张圣君信仰为例 |journal=世界宗教文化 |publication-place=北京 |issue=02 |page=78-81 |issn=1007-6255}}（位于[[闽清县]]）和福建省文物保护单位[[张真君祖殿]]（位于[[台江区]]）{{cite web|publisher= |title=《福建省人民政府关于公布第七批省级文物保护单位名单及保护范围的通知》（闽政文〔2009〕375号） |url=http://www.fujian.gov.cn/zwgk/zxwj/szfwj/200912/t20091209_177441.htm |author=福建省人民政府 |date=2009-11-16 |accessdate=2012-11-12 |deadurl=yes |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131224122739/http://www.fujian.gov.cn/zwgk/zxwj/szfwj/200912/t20091209_177441.htm |archivedate=2013-12-24 }}。 玄天上帝，在霞浦文书中被称作“北方镇天真武菩萨”{{NoteTag|词源自明成祖所加封的“北极镇天真武玄天上帝”}}或“真武大圣”，为[[玄武派]]道教人物，是太上老君第八十二次变化之身，是道教四大护法神（另外三个分别是青龙、白虎和朱雀）之一。在一般的道教寺院中，闾山派和玄武派的人物不能并行出现，而福寿宫则同时供奉了来自两个派系的人物，体现出摩尼教寺院依托道教但又有别于道教的一面。 === 三殿一阁 === 福寿宫拥有四个偏殿，左侧为[[临水夫人]]殿，右侧有[[观音]]阁、[[华光大帝|华光]]殿和[[境主|大王]]殿。依据宫内留存的旧石碑的记载，福寿宫的原址旁建有毓麟宫（供奉临水夫人）和大王殿，而观音阁、华光殿为后续兴建。这些偏殿的神明分别属于[[佛教]]、道教系统。 == 注释 == {{NoteFoot}} == 参见 == {{Commons category}} {{fzcuo|1742|浦西福寿宫}} * [[乐山堂]] * [[塔后三佛塔]] * [[飞路塔]] * [[草庵]] == 参考文献 == {{Reflist}} {{摩尼教页脚}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Pu浦}} [[Category:臺江區建築物]] [[Category:福州道教建筑物]] [[Category:摩尼教建筑物]] [[Category:北宋宗教建筑]] [[Category:祖殿]]

{{Infobox religious building | building_name = 浦西境福寿宫
（明教文佛祖殿） | image = 浦西福寿宫外景 03.jpg | caption = 浦西境福寿宫外景 | coordinates = {{coord|26|3|46.76|N|119|17|23.82|E|scale:10000|display=title,inline}} | mapframe-zoom = 13 | religious_affiliation = [[明教]]（[[摩尼教]]）
[[道教]]、[[中国民间信仰|福建民间信仰]] | heritage = [[台江区]]不可移动文物点 | architecture_type = [[宫庙]] | totalarea = 约900㎡ | location = {{PRC}}[[福建省]][[福州市]][[台江区]][[义洲街道]]浦西村6-1号 }} '''福寿宫'''{{Cite journal |author=林悟殊 |title=福州浦西福寿宫“明教文佛”宗教属性辨析 |journal=中山大学学报（社会科学版） |publication-date=2004 |issue=06 |page=118-123 |issn=1000-9639}}，又称'''浦西境福寿宫'''{{Cite journal |author=彭晓静 |author2=杨富学 |title=福州福寿宫摩尼教神祇探原 |journal=世界宗教研究 |publication-date=2016 |issue=03 |page=118-128 |issn=1000-4289}}、'''福州福寿宫'''{{Cite journal |author=林子周 |title=福建霞浦与福州福寿宫摩尼教文物比较研究 |journal=[[石河子大学]]学报（哲学社会科学版） |publication-place=[[石河子市]] |publication-date=2016 |issue=06 |page=33-39 |doi=10.13880/j.cnki.cn65-1210/c.20161221.010 |issn=1671-0304}}，旧称'''明教文佛祖殿'''，是位于福建省福州市台江区义洲街道浦西社区的一棟宗教建築物，为福州市境内仅存的[[摩尼教]]建筑，有“摩尼教文化的活化石”之称，现为福州市台江区不可移动文物点。{{Cite news|url=http://kejiao.cntv.cn/20120509/100101.shtml|title=台江浦西福寿宫被确认为摩尼教寺院遗址|work=[[中国网络电视台]]|date=2012-05-09|accessdate=2023-10-01|language=zh|archive-date=2021-10-21|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211021213535/http://kejiao.cntv.cn/20120509/100101.shtml|dead-url=no}}浦西福壽宮同時也是[[九案泰山]]信仰中的十三個堂之一。 == 历史 == [[File:浦西福寿宫内殿.jpg|thumb|浦西福寿宫内殿]] 明教文佛祖殿始建于宋代，历年屡经修复与搬迁。依照[[万历]]版[[福宁州]]志中的记载，明教文佛祖殿是为了祭祀北宋[[嘉祐]]年间在[[闽县]]祈雨的摩尼教祖师[[林瞪]]而修建的{{Cite news|title=揭秘“霞浦摩尼教”重大发现|author=茹捷|work=闽东日报|date=2010-04-29|language=zh}}。由于中国历史上（尤其是[[明朝]]时期）对明教大多持禁止态度，当地的萧姓氏族信徒表面上改信[[道教]]，供奉了其他道教、民间信仰神以暗中保护明教文佛的神位{{Cite journal |author=李林洲 |title=福州摩尼教重要遗址——福州台江义洲浦西福寿宫 |journal=福建宗教 |publication-date=2004 |issue=01 |page=43-44}}。1997年及之前，祖殿的原址位于今福州市味精厂，1997年因原址建设味精厂而迁至浦西现址{{Cite journal |author=陈一舟 |author2=涂元济 |title=福建摩尼教寺院遗址考 |journal=海交史研究 |publication-place=[[泉州市]] |publication-date=2004 |issue=01 |page=75-83 |issn=1006-8384}}。由于当时宗教属性不明，1998年，有关部门为令文佛祖殿拥有合法的宗教身份而将其改名为“福寿宫”，并纳入福州的[[道教]]庙宇体系{{Cite book|title=福州市志（1995—2005）|last=福州市地方志编纂委员会|publisher=方志出版社|year=2017|isbn=978-7-5034-9649-3|location=北京|pages=826}}；在2000年代初，由于证据不足，学界仍对福寿宫的宗教属性有极大的争议，直至2008年后[[霞浦文书]]的发现与研究，方确定了福寿宫的宗教属性确为[[摩尼教]]（[[明教]]），且为现今保存最好且配置最完备的摩尼教寺庙。 明教文佛祖殿有一个分殿，位于闽侯县[[上街镇]]石沙自然村，该庙为当地信徒的祖先于200多年前自祖殿分炉而来，庙内祭祀的两个主神明与祖殿相同。 == 建筑 == 明教文佛祖殿1997年及以前的原址坐东朝西，与摩尼教敬西的观念相符，占地3.5亩，建筑面积2亩，包括大殿、配殿、戏台、酒廊看台、厨房和花园等建筑。 浦西境福寿宫的现址总面积900多平方米，坐北朝南。主建筑占地742.6平方米，进深22.6米，大殿宽14.7米，高10米，主要用于祭祀正殿内主供的两尊神：以[[摩尼]]为原型的明教文佛和以福建明教祖师林瞪为原型的度师真君；主殿内主尊左右二侧分别配祀[[张真君]]和[[真武大帝]]，另有三十六护法神将。此外，福寿宫有三座偏殿，分别是临水夫人殿、华光殿和大王殿，供奉[[臨水夫人]]、[[华光大帝]]和黄、赵二大王。 == 神祇系统 == 福寿宫内供奉的各路神祇集摩尼教、[[道教]]、[[佛教]]和当地[[中国民间信仰|民间信仰]]于一宫，也是迄今唯一一座配置完善且香火延续至今的摩尼教寺院。 === 主供神 === 福寿宫主供神为明教文佛和度师真人（又称度师真君），分别代表摩尼教的主神[[摩尼]]（216年—270年代）和[[霞浦县|霞浦]]摩尼教教主林瞪（1003年—1059年）。{{multiple image | align = right | total_width = 260 | image1 = 浦西境福寿宫中的明教文佛.jpg | width1 = 300 | height1 = 350 | caption1 = 明教文佛 | image2 = 浦西境福寿宫中的度师真君.jpg | width2 = 300 | height2 = 350 | caption2 = 度师真君 | caption_align = center }} 在最早的研究中，以[[李林洲]]为代表的学者通过田野调查，以明教与摩尼教等同、且摩尼教寺院草庵中有带有“文佛”楹联等为理据，认为福寿宫中的明教文佛就是摩尼教的主神摩尼佛，而度师真君则应为某个摩尼师；而这一说法由于证据不足在2004年时遭到了以[[林悟殊]]为代表的摩尼教研究学者的反对。林悟殊认为，明教文佛可能应当依照名称解读为“善于教诲、主管文事”的神明；也不能“勉强把度师真人推测为摩尼师”，而应与福寿宫的建造者萧氏宗族有关。 2008年至2009年间，随着霞浦摩尼教文献的发现，对明教文佛祖殿的两尊主神也有了更明晰的认识{{Cite news|title=福州福寿宫与明教关系密切|author=温海龙|work=福州日报|date=2009-11-17|language=zh}}。明教文佛身上的花纹、配饰，以及身后壁画中的许多画面均与霞浦摩尼教艺术品相类似，尤其是文佛头上的太阳花，福州其他的道教寺庙中没有相类似的装饰，而霞浦摩尼教雕塑中有类似的内容，从而证明了明教文佛确实从属于霞浦摩尼教体系。目前，学界针对明教文佛的身份仍有各种不同的推测，除了大部分观点认同的摩尼教主神摩尼之外，也有观点认为明教文佛是“文佛使者”，或与霞浦文书提到的“[[釋迦文佛]]”“[[泗州大聖|泗州文佛]]”有关。{{Cite journal |author=杨富学 |author2=李晓燕 |author3=彭晓静 |title=福建摩尼教遗存踏查之主要收获 |journal=宗教学研究 |publication-place=[[成都市]] |publication-date=2017 |issue=04 |page=259-271 |issn=1006-1312}} 浦西福寿宫张圣真君.jpg | caption1 = 张圣真君 | image2 = 浦西福寿宫玄天上帝.jpg | caption2 = 玄天上帝 | perrow = 2/2 | align = right | total_width = 300 }} 祖殿分别在北壁（主尊所在的方位）西侧和东侧配祀[[张圣真君]]和[[玄天上帝]]，均系道教相关人物。 张圣真君（或称张真君、张圣君）是三十三天监雷法主，出生于[[永泰县|永泰]]，[[閭山派]]祖师[[许真君]]的数传弟子。据上万村村民相传，林瞪曾在闾山修习过许真君所传道法，霞浦文书中也有文献提到闾山派有关的信息，张圣真君以监雷法主的身份配祀的另一个原因则可能是林瞪因祈雨之功被封为“洞天都雷使”{{NoteTag|《兴福祖庆诞科》作“兴福都雷使”，《乐山堂神记》作“感应兴福雷使真君”，《明门初传请本师》作“洞天兴福雷使真君”。}}，与张圣真君的“监雷”身份相关。福州市境内现存有[[张圣真君祖殿]]{{Cite web|date=2023-06-27|title=自然地理|url=http://www.fzmq.gov.cn/xjwz/zjmq/zrdl/201704/t20170413_391146.htm|access-date=2023-10-30|website=闽清县人民政府|archive-date=2022-11-20|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221120015517/http://www.fzmq.gov.cn/xjwz/zjmq/zrdl/201704/t20170413_391146.htm|dead-url=no}}{{Cite |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20131224122739/http://www.fujian.gov.cn/zwgk/zxwj/szfwj/200912/t20091209_177441.htm |archivedate=2013-12-24 }}。 玄天上帝，在霞浦文书中被称作“北方镇天真武菩萨”{{NoteTag|词源自明成祖所加封的“北极镇天真武玄天上帝”}}或“真武大圣”，为[[玄武派]]道教人物，是太上老君第八十二次变化之身，是道教四大护法神（另外三个分别是青龙、白虎和朱雀）之一。在一般的道教寺院中，闾山派和玄武派的人物不能并行出现，而福寿宫则同时供奉了来自两个派系的人物，体现出摩尼教寺院依托道教但又有别于道教的一面。 === 三殿一阁 === 福寿宫拥有四个偏殿，左侧为[[临水夫人]]殿，右侧有[[观音]]阁、[[华光大帝|华光]]殿和[[境主|大王]]殿。依据宫内留存的旧石碑的记载，福寿宫的原址旁建有毓麟宫（供奉临水夫人）和大王殿，而观音阁、华光殿为后续兴建。这些偏殿的神明分别属于[[佛教]]、道教系统。 == 注释 == {{NoteFoot}} == 参见 == {{Commons category}} {{fzcuo|1742|浦西福寿宫}} * [[乐山堂]] * [[塔后三佛塔]] * [[飞路塔]] * [[草庵]] == 参考文献 == {{Reflist}} {{摩尼教页脚}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Pu浦}} [[Category:臺江區建築物]] [[Category:福州道教建筑物]] [[Category:摩尼教建筑物]] [[Category:北宋宗教建筑]] [[Category:祖殿]] [[Category:福建省未定级不可移动文物]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | building_name = Puxi Fushou Palace
（Mingjiao Wenfó Patriarch Hall） | image = 浦西福寿宫外景 03.jpg | caption = Exterior view of Puxi Fushou Palace | coordinates = {{coord|26|3|46.76|N|119|17|23.82|E|scale:10000|display=title,inline}} | mapframe-zoom = 13 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Manichaeism|de|Manichäismus|ja|明教|zh|明教}}（{{ill|Manichaeism|de|Manichäismus|ja|摩尼教|zh|摩尼教}}）
{{ill|Taoism|de|Daoismus|ja|道教|zh|道教}}、{{ill|Chinese Folk Religion|de|Chinesischer Volksglaube|ja|中国民間信仰|zh|中国民间信仰|lt=Fujian Folk Religion|lt_ja=福建民間信仰|lt_de=Fujian Volksglaube|lt_zh=福建民间信仰}} | heritage = {{ill|Taijiang District|de|Taijiang Bezirk|ja|台江区|zh|台江区}} Immovable Cultural Relic | architecture_type = {{ill|Temple|de|Tempel|ja|宮廟|zh|宫庙}} | totalarea = Approximately 900㎡ | location = {{PRC}}{{ill|Fujian Province|de|Fujian Provinz|ja|福建省|zh|福建省}}{{ill|Fuzhou|de|Fuzhou|ja|福州市|zh|福州市}}{{ill|Taijiang District|de|Taijiang Bezirk|ja|台江区|zh|台江区}}{{ill|Yizhou Subdistrict|de|Yizhou Stadtteil|ja|義洲街道|zh|义洲街道}} Puxi Village 6-1 }} '''Fushou Palace'''{{Cite journal |author=Lin Wushu |title=An Analysis of the Religious Attributes of “Mingjiao Wenfó” at Puxi Fushou Palace in Fuzhou |journal=Journal of Sun Yat-sen University (Social Sciences Edition) |publication-date=2004 |issue=06 |page=118-123 |issn=1000-9639}}, also known as '''Puxi Region Fushou Palace'''{{Cite journal |author=Peng Xiaojing |author2=Yang Fuxue |title=An Investigation into the Manichaean Deities at Fushou Palace in Fuzhou |journal=World Religious Studies |publication-date=2016 |issue=03 |page=118-128 |issn=1000-4289}} and '''Fuzhou Fushou Palace'''{{Cite journal |author=Lin Zizhou |title=A Comparative Study of Manichaean Relics between Xiapu and Fushou Palace in Fuzhou |journal={{ill|Shihezi University|de|Shihezi University|ja|石河子大学|zh|石河子大学}} Journal (Philosophy and Social Sciences Edition) |publication-place={{ill|Shihezi City|de|Stadt Shihezi|ja|石河子市|zh|石河子市}} |publication-date=2016 |issue=06 |page=33-39 |doi=10.13880/j.cnki.cn65-1210/c.20161221.010 |issn=1671-0304}}, formerly known as '''Mingjiao Wenfó Patriarch Hall''', is a religious building located in the Puxi community of {{ill|Fuzhou|de|Fuzhou|ja|福州市|zh|福州市}}, {{ill|Taijiang District|de|Taijiang Bezirk|ja|台江区|zh|台江区}}, {{ill|Yizhou Subdistrict|de|Yizhou Stadtteil|ja|義洲街道|zh|义洲街道}} in {{ill|Fujian Province|de|Fujian Provinz|ja|福建省|zh|福建省}}. It is the only remaining {{ill|Manichaeism|de|Manichäismus|ja|摩尼教|zh|摩尼教}} building in Fuzhou and is known as a “living fossil of Manichaean culture”. It is currently an immovable cultural relic site in Taijiang District, Fuzhou.{{Cite news|url=http://kejiao.cntv.cn/20120509/100101.shtml|title=Puxi Fushou Palace in Taijiang Confirmed as a Manichaean Temple Ruin|work={{ill|China Network Television|de|China Network Television|ja|中国民間テレビ局|zh|中国网络电视台}}|date=2012-05-09|accessdate=2023-10-01|language=zh|archive-date=2021-10-21|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211021213535/http://kejiao.cntv.cn/20120509/100101.shtml|dead-url=no}} Puxi Fushou Palace is also one of the thirteen halls in the faith of {{ill|Jiu'an Taishan|de|Jiu'an Taishan|ja|九案泰山|zh|九案泰山}}. == History == [[File:浦西福寿宫内殿.jpg|thumb|Interior Hall of Puxi Fushou Palace]] The Mingjiao Wenfó Patriarch Hall was originally built during the Song Dynasty and has undergone multiple restorations and relocations over the years. According to the records in the {{ill|Wanli|de|Wanli|ja|万曆|zh|万历}} edition of the {{ill|Funing Prefecture|de|Funing Präfektur|ja|福寧州|zh|福宁州}} Gazetteer, the hall was constructed to commemorate the Manichaean patriarch {{ill|Lin Deng|de|Lin Deng|ja|林瞪|zh|林瞪}}, who prayed for rain in {{ill|Min County|de|Min Landkreis|ja|閩縣|zh|闽县}} during the {{ill|Jiayou|de|Jiayou|ja|嘉祐|zh|嘉祐}} era of the Northern Song. Due to the generally prohibitive attitude toward Mingjiao during Chinese history (especially in the {{ill|Ming Dynasty|de|Ming-Dynasty|ja|明朝|zh|明朝}}), local believers of the Xiao clan outwardly converted to {{ill|Taoism|de|Daoismus|ja|道教|zh|道教}}, enshrining other Taoist and folk deities to secretly safeguard the deity of Mingjiao Wenfó{{Cite journal |author=Li Linzhou |title=An Important Manichaean Site in Fuzhou — Fushou Palace in Yizhou, Taijiang, Fuzhou |journal=Fujian Religion |publication-date=2004 |issue=01 |page=43-44}}. Until 1997, the original site of the ancestral hall was located at the present-day Fuzhou MSG Factory; in 1997, due to the construction of the factory on that site, it was relocated to its current location in Puxi{{Cite journal |author=Chen Yizhou |author2=Tu Yuanji |title=A Study on the Manichaean Temple Ruins in Fujian |journal=Maritime History Research |publication-place={{ill|Quanzhou|de|Quanzhou|ja|泉州市|zh|泉州市}} |publication-date=2004 |issue=01 |page=75-83 |issn=1006-8384}}. Owing to the unclear religious affiliation at the time, in 1998 the authorities renamed Wenfó Patriarch Hall as “Fushou Palace” to grant it a legitimate religious status and incorporated it into Fuzhou’s {{ill|Taoism|de|Daoismus|ja|道教|zh|道教}} temple system{{Cite book|title=Fuzhou Annals (1995—2005)|last=Fuzhou Local Annals Compilation Committee|publisher=Fangzhi Press|year=2017|isbn=978-7-5034-9649-3|location=Beijing|pages=826}}; in the early 2000s, due to insufficient evidence, there remained considerable academic debate over its religious affiliation, until after the discovery and study of the {{ill|Xiapu Documents|de|Xiapu Dokumente|ja|霞浦文書|zh|霞浦文书}} in 2008 confirmed that Fushou Palace is indeed a {{ill|Manichaeism|de|Manichäismus|ja|摩尼教|zh|摩尼教}} ({{ill|Mingjiao|de|Mingjiao|ja|明教|zh|明教}}) temple – the best-preserved and most fully equipped of its kind today. The Mingjiao Wenfó Patriarch Hall also has a subsidiary hall located in Shisha Natural Village, {{ill|Shangjie Town|de|Shangjie Town|ja|上街鎮|zh|上街镇}}, Minhou County. This temple originated over 200 years ago when local believers’ ancestors branched off from the ancestral hall; the two main deities enshrined here are identical to those of the main hall. == Architecture == The original site of the Mingjiao Wenfó Patriarch Hall (up to 1997) faced east with its entrance oriented westward, in line with the Manichaean concept of venerating the west. It covered an area of 3.5 mu with a building area of 2 mu, comprising a main hall, an auxiliary hall, a performance stage, a banquet viewing platform, a kitchen, and a garden. The current site of Puxi Fushou Palace spans over 900 square meters and faces south. The main building occupies 742.6 square meters, has a depth of 22.6 meters, a main hall width of 14.7 meters, and a height of 10 meters. It primarily enshrines two principal deities: the Mingjiao Wenfó modeled after {{ill|Mani|de|Mani|ja|摩尼|zh|摩尼}}, and Dushi Zhenjun modeled after the Fujian Manichaean patriarch {{ill|Lin Deng|de|Lin Deng|ja|林瞪|zh|林瞪}}. In the main hall, flanking the principal deity on its left and right are the auxiliary deities {{ill|Zhang Zhenjun|de|Zhang Zhenjun|ja|張真君|zh|张真君}} and {{ill|Zhenwu Dadi|de|Zhenwu Dadi|ja|真武大帝|zh|真武大帝}}, respectively, along with thirty-six guardian deities. In addition, Fushou Palace has three subsidiary halls – the {{ill|Linshui Furen Hall|de|Linshui Furen Hall|ja|臨水夫人殿|zh|临水夫人殿}}, the {{ill|Huaguang Hall|de|Huaguang Hall|ja|华光殿|zh|华光殿}}, and the {{ill|Jingzhu|de|Jingzhu|ja|境主|zh|境主|lt=Dawang|lt_ja=大王|lt_de=Dawang|lt_zh=大王}} Hall – which enshrine {{ill|Linshui Furen|de|Linshui Furen|ja|臨水夫人|zh|臨水夫人}}, {{ill|Huaguang Dadi|de|Huaguang Dadi|ja|华光大帝|zh|华光大帝}} and the two kings, Huang and Zhao, respectively. == Deity System == The deities enshrined within Fushou Palace comprise figures from Manichaeism, {{ill|Taoism|de|Daoismus|ja|道教|zh|道教}}, {{ill|Buddhism|de|Buddhismus|ja|佛教|zh|佛教}}, and local {{ill|Chinese Folk Religion|de|Chinesischer Volksglaube|ja|中国民間信仰|zh|中国民间信仰|lt=Folk Religion|lt_ja=民間信仰|lt_de=Volksglaube|lt_zh=民间信仰}}, all merged within one palace. It is also the only Manichaean temple with a complete configuration and continuously maintained incense traditions to this day. === Main Deities === The principal deities of Fushou Palace are Mingjiao Wenfó and Dushi Zhenren (also known as Dushi Zhenjun), representing respectively the primary deity of Manichaeism, {{ill|Mani|de|Mani|ja|摩尼|zh|摩尼}} (216–270 AD), and the Xiapu Manichaean leader {{ill|Lin Deng|de|Lin Deng|ja|林瞪|zh|林瞪}} (1003–1059 AD). {{multiple image | align = right | total_width = 260 | image1 = 浦西境福寿宫中的明教文佛.jpg | width1 = 300 | height1 = 350 | caption1 = Mingjiao Wenfó | image2 = 浦西境福寿宫中的度师真君.jpg | width2 = 300 | height2 = 350 | caption2 = Dushi Zhenjun | caption_align = center }} In the earliest studies, scholars such as {{ill|Li Linzhou|de|Li Linzhou|ja|李林洲|zh|李林洲}} conducted field research and, equating Mingjiao with Manichaeism – citing the presence of “Wenfó” couplets in the modest lodgings of Manichaean temples – argued that the Mingjiao Wenfó in Fushou Palace was in fact the chief deity Mani Buddha of Manichaeism, with Dushi Zhenjun representing a certain Manichaean master. However, due to insufficient evidence, this view was challenged in 2004 by Manichaean scholars such as {{ill|Lin Wushu|de|Lin Wushu|ja|林悟殊|zh|林悟殊}}. Between 2008 and 2009, with the discovery of the {{ill|Xiapu Documents|de|Xiapu Dokumente|ja|霞浦文書|zh|霞浦文书}}, a clearer understanding of the two main deities of the Mingjiao Wenfó Patriarch Hall emerged{{Cite news|title=Fuzhou Fushou Palace Has a Close Relationship with Mingjiao|author=Wen Hailong|work=Fuzhou Daily|date=2009-11-17|language=zh}}. The patterns and adornments on Mingjiao Wenfó, as well as many scenes depicted in the murals behind him – notably the sunburst on his head – resemble those found in Xiapu Manichaean artworks (a decorative element absent in other Taoist temples in Fuzhou), thereby confirming his affiliation with the Xiapu Manichaean system. At present, academic opinions on the identity of Mingjiao Wenfó vary; aside from the prevailing view that he is the Manichaean chief deity Mani, some scholars suggest that he may be a “Wenfó envoy” or related to what the Xiapu documents refer to as {{ill|Shakyamuni Wenfó|de|Shakyamuni Wenfó|ja|釈迦文佛|zh|釋迦文佛}} and {{ill|Sizhou Great Saint|de|Sizhou Großer Heiliger|ja|泗州大聖|zh|泗州大聖|lt=Sizhou Wenfó|lt_ja=泗州文佛|lt_de=Sizhou Wenfó|lt_zh=泗州文佛}}.{{Cite journal |author=Yang Fuxue |author2=Li Xiaoyan |author3=Peng Xiaojing |title=Major Findings from the Survey of Manichaean Relics in Fujian |journal=Studies in Religious Research |publication-place={{ill|Chengdu|de|Chengdu|ja|成都市|zh|成都市}} |publication-date=2017 |issue=04 |page=259-271 |issn=1006-1312}} === Ancillary Deities in the Main Hall === {{Multiple image | image1 = 浦西福寿宫张圣真君.jpg | caption1 = Zhang Zhenjun | image2 = 浦西福寿宫玄天上帝.jpg | caption2 = Xuántiān Shangdi | perrow = 2/2 | align = right | total_width = 300 }} The ancestral hall enshrines, on the west and east sides of its north wall (the side where the principal deity is located), the deities {{ill|Zhang Zhenjun|de|Zhang Zhenjun|ja|張真君|zh|张圣真君}} and {{ill|Xuántiān Shangdi|de|Xuántiān Shangdi|ja|玄天上帝|zh|玄天上帝}}, both figures associated with Taoism. {{NoteTag|Zhang Zhenjun (also known as Zhang Zhenjun or Zhang Shengjun) is the chief of the Thunder Regulation of the Thirty-Three Heavens, born in {{ill|Yongtai County|de|Yongtai County|ja|永泰县|zh|永泰县}}, and a direct disciple of {{ill|Xu Zhenjun|de|Xu Zhenjun|ja|許真君|zh|许真君}} of the Lushan Sect. Local tradition holds that Lin Deng once studied the Daoist techniques transmitted by Xu Zhenjun on Lushan. Additionally, the Xiapu Documents mention information related to the Lushan Sect. Another reason for enshrining Zhang Zhenjun may be that Lin Deng was conferred the title “Dongtian Dulei Shi” (as rendered in various sources) in recognition of his merit in praying for rain.}} According to local tradition, Zhang Zhenjun is the Thunder Regulation Chief; indeed, Fuzhou still preserves the {{ill|Zhang Zhenjun Ancestral Hall|de|Zhang Zhenjun Ancestral Hall|ja|張聖真君祖殿|zh|张圣真君祖殿}} in {{ill|Minqing County|de|Minqing County|ja|闽清县|zh|闽清县}}{{Cite web|date=2023-06-27|title=Physical Geography|url=http://www.fzmq.gov.cn/xjwz/zjmq/zrdl/201704/t20170413_391146.htm|access-date=2023-10-30|website=Minqing County Government|archive-date=2022-11-20|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221120015517/http://www.fzmq.gov.cn/xjwz/zjmq/zrdl/201704/t20170413_391146.htm|dead-url=no}} and the Fujian Provincial Cultural Heritage Unit {{ill|Zhang Zhenjun Ancestral Hall|de|Zhang Zhenjun Ancestral Hall|ja|張真君祖殿|zh|张真君祖殿}} (located in {{ill|Taijiang District|de|Taijiang Bezirk|ja|台江区|zh|台江区}}). Xuántiān Shangdi, as recorded in the Xiapu Documents, is referred to as “Northern Guardian Zhenwu Bodhisattva”{{NoteTag|Term derived from the title conferred by Emperor Yongle: “Northern Polar Guardian Zhenwu Xuántiān Shangdi”}} or “Great Saint Zhenwu”. He is a Taoist figure of the {{ill|Xuanwu Sect|de|Xuanwu Sect|ja|玄武派|zh|玄武派}}, regarded as the 82nd transformation of the Supreme Elderly Lord and one of Taoism’s four major guardian deities (the others being the Azure Dragon, White Tiger, and Vermilion Bird). In most Taoist temples, figures from the Lushan and Xuanwu sects do not coexist; however, Fushou Palace enshrines deities from both, reflecting its unique position as a Manichaean temple that both draws on and distinguishes itself from Taoism. === Three Halls and One Pavilion === Fushou Palace features four subsidiary halls: on the left is the {{ill|Linshui Furen Hall|de|Linshui Furen Hall|ja|臨水夫人殿|zh|临水夫人殿}}, while on the right are the {{ill|Guanyin Pavilion|de|Guanyin Pavillon|ja|観音閣|zh|观音阁}}, the hall of {{ill|Huaguang Dadi|de|Huaguang Dadi|ja|华光大帝|zh|华光大帝|lt=Huaguang|lt_ja=华光|lt_de=Huaguang|lt_zh=华光}}, and the hall of {{ill|Jingzhu|de|Jingzhu|ja|境主|zh|境主|lt=Dawang|lt_ja=大王|lt_de=Dawang|lt_zh=Dawang}}. According to inscriptions on old stone tablets preserved within the palace, adjacent to the original site of Fushou Palace were the Yulin Palace (dedicated to Linshui Furen) and Dawang Hall, while the Guanyin Pavilion and Huaguang Hall were later additions. The deities in these halls belong respectively to the {{ill|Buddhism|de|Buddhismus|ja|佛教|zh|佛教}} and Taoist systems. == Notes == {{NoteFoot}} == See Also == {{Commons category}} {{fzcuo|1742|Puxi Fushou Palace}} * {{ill|Leshantang|de|Leshantang|ja|乐山堂|zh|乐山堂}} * {{ill|Tahou Third Buddha Pagoda|de|Tahou Third Buddha Pagoda|ja|塔后三佛塔|zh|塔后三佛塔}} * {{ill|Feilu Pagoda|de|Feilu Pagoda|ja|飞路塔|zh|飞路塔}} * {{ill|Cao'an|de|Cao'an|ja|草庵|zh|草庵}}[[zh:浦西境福寿宫]] == References == {{Reflist}} {{Manichaeism footer}} [[Category:Buildings in Taijiang District]] [[Category:Taoist Buildings in Fuzhou]] [[Category:Manichaean Buildings]] [[Category:Religious Buildings of the Northern Song Dynasty]] [[Category:Ancestral Halls]] [[Category:Undesignated Immovable Cultural Relics in Fujian Province]] [[Category:completion tier 1]][[ja:浦西福寿宮]][[de:Puxi Fushou Palace]] {{Manichaeism}}

{{Infobox religious building | building_name = Puxi Fushou Palace
（Mingjiao Wenfó Patriarch Hall） | image = 浦西福寿宫外景 03.jpg | caption = Exterior view of Puxi Fushou Palace | coordinates = {{coord|26|3|46.76|N|119|17|23.82|E|scale:10000|display=title,inline}} | mapframe-zoom = 13 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Manichaeism|de|Manichäismus|ja|明教|zh|明教}}（{{ill|Manichaeism|de|Manichäismus|ja|摩尼教|zh|摩尼教}}）
{{ill|Taoism|de|Daoismus|ja|道教|zh|道教}}、{{ill|Chinese Folk Religion|de|Chinesischer Volksglaube|ja|中国民間信仰|zh|中国民间信仰|lt=Fujian Folk Religion|lt_ja=福建民間信仰|lt_de=Fujian Volksglaube|lt_zh=福建民间信仰}} | heritage = {{ill|Taijiang District|de|Taijiang Bezirk|ja|台江区|zh|台江区}} Immovable Cultural Relic | architecture_type = {{ill|Temple|de|Tempel|ja|宮廟|zh|宫庙}} | totalarea = Approximately 900㎡ | location = {{PRC}}{{ill|Fujian Province|de|Fujian Provinz|ja|福建省|zh|福建省}}{{ill|Fuzhou|de|Fuzhou|ja|福州市|zh|福州市}}{{ill|Taijiang District|de|Taijiang Bezirk|ja|台江区|zh|台江区}}{{ill|Yizhou Subdistrict|de|Yizhou Stadtteil|ja|義洲街道|zh|义洲街道}} Puxi Village 6-1 }} '''Fushou Palace'''{{Cite journal |author=Lin Wushu |title=An Analysis of the Religious Attributes of “Mingjiao Wenfó” at Puxi Fushou Palace in Fuzhou |journal=Journal of Sun Yat-sen University (Social Sciences Edition) |publication-date=2004 |issue=06 |page=118-123 |issn=1000-9639}}, also known as '''Puxi Region Fushou Palace'''{{Cite journal |author=Peng Xiaojing |author2=Yang Fuxue |title=An Investigation into the Manichaean Deities at Fushou Palace in Fuzhou |journal=World Religious Studies |publication-date=2016 |issue=03 |page=118-128 |issn=1000-4289}} and '''Fuzhou Fushou Palace'''{{Cite journal |author=Lin Zizhou |title=A Comparative Study of Manichaean Relics between Xiapu and Fushou Palace in Fuzhou |journal={{ill|Shihezi University|de|Shihezi University|ja|石河子大学|zh|石河子大学}} Journal (Philosophy and Social Sciences Edition) |publication-place={{ill|Shihezi City|de|Stadt Shihezi|ja|石河子市|zh|石河子市}} |publication-date=2016 |issue=06 |page=33-39 |doi=10.13880/j.cnki.cn65-1210/c.20161221.010 |issn=1671-0304}}, formerly known as '''Mingjiao Wenfó Patriarch Hall''', is a religious building located in the Puxi community of {{ill|Fuzhou|de|Fuzhou|ja|福州市|zh|福州市}}, {{ill|Taijiang District|de|Taijiang Bezirk|ja|台江区|zh|台江区}}, {{ill|Yizhou Subdistrict|de|Yizhou Stadtteil|ja|義洲街道|zh|义洲街道}} in {{ill|Fujian Province|de|Fujian Provinz|ja|福建省|zh|福建省}}. It is the only remaining {{ill|Manichaeism|de|Manichäismus|ja|摩尼教|zh|摩尼教}} building in Fuzhou and is known as a “living fossil of Manichaean culture”. It is currently an immovable cultural relic site in Taijiang District, Fuzhou.{{Cite news|url=http://kejiao.cntv.cn/20120509/100101.shtml|title=Puxi Fushou Palace in Taijiang Confirmed as a Manichaean Temple Ruin|work={{ill|China Network Television|de|China Network Television|ja|中国民間テレビ局|zh|中国网络电视台}}|date=2012-05-09|accessdate=2023-10-01|language=zh|archive-date=2021-10-21|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211021213535/http://kejiao.cntv.cn/20120509/100101.shtml|dead-url=no}} Puxi Fushou Palace is also one of the thirteen halls in the faith of {{ill|Jiu'an Taishan|de|Jiu'an Taishan|ja|九案泰山|zh|九案泰山}}. == History == [[File:浦西福寿宫内殿.jpg|thumb|Interior Hall of Puxi Fushou Palace]] The Mingjiao Wenfó Patriarch Hall was originally built during the Song Dynasty and has undergone multiple restorations and relocations over the years. According to the records in the {{ill|Wanli|de|Wanli|ja|万曆|zh|万历}} edition of the {{ill|Funing Prefecture|de|Funing Präfektur|ja|福寧州|zh|福宁州}} Gazetteer, the hall was constructed to commemorate the Manichaean patriarch {{ill|Lin Deng|de|Lin Deng|ja|林瞪|zh|林瞪}}, who prayed for rain in {{ill|Min County|de|Min Landkreis|ja|閩縣|zh|闽县}} during the {{ill|Jiayou|de|Jiayou|ja|嘉祐|zh|嘉祐}} era of the Northern Song. Due to the generally prohibitive attitude toward Mingjiao during Chinese history (especially in the {{ill|Ming Dynasty|de|Ming-Dynasty|ja|明朝|zh|明朝}}), local believers of the Xiao clan outwardly converted to {{ill|Taoism|de|Daoismus|ja|道教|zh|道教}}, enshrining other Taoist and folk deities to secretly safeguard the deity of Mingjiao Wenfó{{Cite journal |author=Li Linzhou |title=An Important Manichaean Site in Fuzhou — Fushou Palace in Yizhou, Taijiang, Fuzhou |journal=Fujian Religion |publication-date=2004 |issue=01 |page=43-44}}. Until 1997, the original site of the ancestral hall was located at the present-day Fuzhou MSG Factory; in 1997, due to the construction of the factory on that site, it was relocated to its current location in Puxi{{Cite journal |author=Chen Yizhou |author2=Tu Yuanji |title=A Study on the Manichaean Temple Ruins in Fujian |journal=Maritime History Research |publication-place={{ill|Quanzhou|de|Quanzhou|ja|泉州市|zh|泉州市}} |publication-date=2004 |issue=01 |page=75-83 |issn=1006-8384}}. Owing to the unclear religious affiliation at the time, in 1998 the authorities renamed Wenfó Patriarch Hall as “Fushou Palace” to grant it a legitimate religious status and incorporated it into Fuzhou’s {{ill|Taoism|de|Daoismus|ja|道教|zh|道教}} temple system{{Cite book|title=Fuzhou Annals (1995—2005)|last=Fuzhou Local Annals Compilation Committee|publisher=Fangzhi Press|year=2017|isbn=978-7-5034-9649-3|location=Beijing|pages=826}}; in the early 2000s, due to insufficient evidence, there remained considerable academic debate over its religious affiliation, until after the discovery and study of the {{ill|Xiapu Documents|de|Xiapu Dokumente|ja|霞浦文書|zh|霞浦文书}} in 2008 confirmed that Fushou Palace is indeed a {{ill|Manichaeism|de|Manichäismus|ja|摩尼教|zh|摩尼教}} ({{ill|Mingjiao|de|Mingjiao|ja|明教|zh|明教}}) temple – the best-preserved and most fully equipped of its kind today. The Mingjiao Wenfó Patriarch Hall also has a subsidiary hall located in Shisha Natural Village, {{ill|Shangjie Town|de|Shangjie Town|ja|上街鎮|zh|上街镇}}, Minhou County. This temple originated over 200 years ago when local believers’ ancestors branched off from the ancestral hall; the two main deities enshrined here are identical to those of the main hall. == Architecture == The original site of the Mingjiao Wenfó Patriarch Hall (up to 1997) faced east with its entrance oriented westward, in line with the Manichaean concept of venerating the west. It covered an area of 3.5 mu with a building area of 2 mu, comprising a main hall, an auxiliary hall, a performance stage, a banquet viewing platform, a kitchen, and a garden. The current site of Puxi Fushou Palace spans over 900 square meters and faces south. The main building occupies 742.6 square meters, has a depth of 22.6 meters, a main hall width of 14.7 meters, and a height of 10 meters. It primarily enshrines two principal deities: the Mingjiao Wenfó modeled after {{ill|Mani|de|Mani|ja|摩尼|zh|摩尼}}, and Dushi Zhenjun modeled after the Fujian Manichaean patriarch {{ill|Lin Deng|de|Lin Deng|ja|林瞪|zh|林瞪}}. In the main hall, flanking the principal deity on its left and right are the auxiliary deities {{ill|Zhang Zhenjun|de|Zhang Zhenjun|ja|張真君|zh|张真君}} and {{ill|Zhenwu Dadi|de|Zhenwu Dadi|ja|真武大帝|zh|真武大帝}}, respectively, along with thirty-six guardian deities. In addition, Fushou Palace has three subsidiary halls – the {{ill|Linshui Furen Hall|de|Linshui Furen Hall|ja|臨水夫人殿|zh|临水夫人殿}}, the {{ill|Huaguang Hall|de|Huaguang Hall|ja|华光殿|zh|华光殿}}, and the {{ill|Jingzhu|de|Jingzhu|ja|境主|zh|境主|lt=Dawang|lt_ja=大王|lt_de=Dawang|lt_zh=大王}} Hall – which enshrine {{ill|Linshui Furen|de|Linshui Furen|ja|臨水夫人|zh|臨水夫人}}, {{ill|Huaguang Dadi|de|Huaguang Dadi|ja|华光大帝|zh|华光大帝}} and the two kings, Huang and Zhao, respectively. == Deity System == The deities enshrined within Fushou Palace comprise figures from Manichaeism, {{ill|Taoism|de|Daoismus|ja|道教|zh|道教}}, {{ill|Buddhism|de|Buddhismus|ja|佛教|zh|佛教}}, and local {{ill|Chinese Folk Religion|de|Chinesischer Volksglaube|ja|中国民間信仰|zh|中国民间信仰|lt=Folk Religion|lt_ja=民間信仰|lt_de=Volksglaube|lt_zh=民间信仰}}, all merged within one palace. It is also the only Manichaean temple with a complete configuration and continuously maintained incense traditions to this day. === Main Deities === The principal deities of Fushou Palace are Mingjiao Wenfó and Dushi Zhenren (also known as Dushi Zhenjun), representing respectively the primary deity of Manichaeism, {{ill|Mani|de|Mani|ja|摩尼|zh|摩尼}} (216–270 AD), and the Xiapu Manichaean leader {{ill|Lin Deng|de|Lin Deng|ja|林瞪|zh|林瞪}} (1003–1059 AD). {{multiple image | align = right | total_width = 260 | image1 = 浦西境福寿宫中的明教文佛.jpg | width1 = 300 | height1 = 350 | caption1 = Mingjiao Wenfó | image2 = 浦西境福寿宫中的度师真君.jpg | width2 = 300 | height2 = 350 | caption2 = Dushi Zhenjun | caption_align = center }} In the earliest studies, scholars such as {{ill|Li Linzhou|de|Li Linzhou|ja|李林洲|zh|李林洲}} conducted field research and, equating Mingjiao with Manichaeism – citing the presence of “Wenfó” couplets in the modest lodgings of Manichaean temples – argued that the Mingjiao Wenfó in Fushou Palace was in fact the chief deity Mani Buddha of Manichaeism, with Dushi Zhenjun representing a certain Manichaean master. However, due to insufficient evidence, this view was challenged in 2004 by Manichaean scholars such as {{ill|Lin Wushu|de|Lin Wushu|ja|林悟殊|zh|林悟殊}}. Between 2008 and 2009, with the discovery of the {{ill|Xiapu Documents|de|Xiapu Dokumente|ja|霞浦文書|zh|霞浦文书}}, a clearer understanding of the two main deities of the Mingjiao Wenfó Patriarch Hall emerged{{Cite news|title=Fuzhou Fushou Palace Has a Close Relationship with Mingjiao|author=Wen Hailong|work=Fuzhou Daily|date=2009-11-17|language=zh}}. The patterns and adornments on Mingjiao Wenfó, as well as many scenes depicted in the murals behind him – notably the sunburst on his head – resemble those found in Xiapu Manichaean artworks (a decorative element absent in other Taoist temples in Fuzhou), thereby confirming his affiliation with the Xiapu Manichaean system. At present, academic opinions on the identity of Mingjiao Wenfó vary; aside from the prevailing view that he is the Manichaean chief deity Mani, some scholars suggest that he may be a “Wenfó envoy” or related to what the Xiapu documents refer to as {{ill|Shakyamuni Wenfó|de|Shakyamuni Wenfó|ja|釈迦文佛|zh|釋迦文佛}} and {{ill|Sizhou Great Saint|de|Sizhou Großer Heiliger|ja|泗州大聖|zh|泗州大聖|lt=Sizhou Wenfó|lt_ja=泗州文佛|lt_de=Sizhou Wenfó|lt_zh=泗州文佛}}.{{Cite journal |author=Yang Fuxue |author2=Li Xiaoyan |author3=Peng Xiaojing |title=Major Findings from the Survey of Manichaean Relics in Fujian |journal=Studies in Religious Research |publication-place={{ill|Chengdu|de|Chengdu|ja|成都市|zh|成都市}} |publication-date=2017 |issue=04 |page=259-271 |issn=1006-1312}} === Ancillary Deities in the Main Hall === {{Multiple image | image1 = 浦西福寿宫张圣真君.jpg | caption1 = Zhang Zhenjun | image2 = 浦西福寿宫玄天上帝.jpg | caption2 = Xuántiān Shangdi | perrow = 2/2 | align = right | total_width = 300 }} The ancestral hall enshrines, on the west and east sides of its north wall (the side where the principal deity is located), the deities {{ill|Zhang Zhenjun|de|Zhang Zhenjun|ja|張真君|zh|张圣真君}} and {{ill|Xuántiān Shangdi|de|Xuántiān Shangdi|ja|玄天上帝|zh|玄天上帝}}, both figures associated with Taoism. {{NoteTag|Zhang Zhenjun (also known as Zhang Zhenjun or Zhang Shengjun) is the chief of the Thunder Regulation of the Thirty-Three Heavens, born in {{ill|Yongtai County|de|Yongtai County|ja|永泰县|zh|永泰县}}, and a direct disciple of {{ill|Xu Zhenjun|de|Xu Zhenjun|ja|許真君|zh|许真君}} of the Lushan Sect. Local tradition holds that Lin Deng once studied the Daoist techniques transmitted by Xu Zhenjun on Lushan. Additionally, the Xiapu Documents mention information related to the Lushan Sect. Another reason for enshrining Zhang Zhenjun may be that Lin Deng was conferred the title “Dongtian Dulei Shi” (as rendered in various sources) in recognition of his merit in praying for rain.}} According to local tradition, Zhang Zhenjun is the Thunder Regulation Chief; indeed, Fuzhou still preserves the {{ill|Zhang Zhenjun Ancestral Hall|de|Zhang Zhenjun Ancestral Hall|ja|張聖真君祖殿|zh|张圣真君祖殿}} in {{ill|Minqing County|de|Minqing County|ja|闽清县|zh|闽清县}}{{Cite web|date=2023-06-27|title=Physical Geography|url=http://www.fzmq.gov.cn/xjwz/zjmq/zrdl/201704/t20170413_391146.htm|access-date=2023-10-30|website=Minqing County Government|archive-date=2022-11-20|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221120015517/http://www.fzmq.gov.cn/xjwz/zjmq/zrdl/201704/t20170413_391146.htm|dead-url=no}} and the Fujian Provincial Cultural Heritage Unit {{ill|Zhang Zhenjun Ancestral Hall|de|Zhang Zhenjun Ancestral Hall|ja|張真君祖殿|zh|张真君祖殿}} (located in {{ill|Taijiang District|de|Taijiang Bezirk|ja|台江区|zh|台江区}}). Xuántiān Shangdi, as recorded in the Xiapu Documents, is referred to as “Northern Guardian Zhenwu Bodhisattva”{{NoteTag|Term derived from the title conferred by Emperor Yongle: “Northern Polar Guardian Zhenwu Xuántiān Shangdi”}} or “Great Saint Zhenwu”. He is a Taoist figure of the {{ill|Xuanwu Sect|de|Xuanwu Sect|ja|玄武派|zh|玄武派}}, regarded as the 82nd transformation of the Supreme Elderly Lord and one of Taoism’s four major guardian deities (the others being the Azure Dragon, White Tiger, and Vermilion Bird). In most Taoist temples, figures from the Lushan and Xuanwu sects do not coexist; however, Fushou Palace enshrines deities from both, reflecting its unique position as a Manichaean temple that both draws on and distinguishes itself from Taoism. === Three Halls and One Pavilion === Fushou Palace features four subsidiary halls: on the left is the {{ill|Linshui Furen Hall|de|Linshui Furen Hall|ja|臨水夫人殿|zh|临水夫人殿}}, while on the right are the {{ill|Guanyin Pavilion|de|Guanyin Pavillon|ja|観音閣|zh|观音阁}}, the hall of {{ill|Huaguang Dadi|de|Huaguang Dadi|ja|华光大帝|zh|华光大帝|lt=Huaguang|lt_ja=华光|lt_de=Huaguang|lt_zh=华光}}, and the hall of {{ill|Jingzhu|de|Jingzhu|ja|境主|zh|境主|lt=Dawang|lt_ja=大王|lt_de=Dawang|lt_zh=Dawang}}. According to inscriptions on old stone tablets preserved within the palace, adjacent to the original site of Fushou Palace were the Yulin Palace (dedicated to Linshui Furen) and Dawang Hall, while the Guanyin Pavilion and Huaguang Hall were later additions. The deities in these halls belong respectively to the {{ill|Buddhism|de|Buddhismus|ja|佛教|zh|佛教}} and Taoist systems. == Notes == {{NoteFoot}} == See Also == {{Commons category}} {{fzcuo|1742|Puxi Fushou Palace}} * {{ill|Leshantang|de|Leshantang|ja|乐山堂|zh|乐山堂}} * {{ill|Tahou Third Buddha Pagoda|de|Tahou Third Buddha Pagoda|ja|塔后三佛塔|zh|塔后三佛塔}} * {{ill|Feilu Pagoda|de|Feilu Pagoda|ja|飞路塔|zh|飞路塔}} * {{ill|Cao'an|de|Cao'an|ja|草庵|zh|草庵}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Manichaeism footer}} {{Manichaeism}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buildings in Taijiang District]] [[Category:Taoist Buildings in Fuzhou]] [[Category:Manichaean Buildings]] [[Category:Religious Buildings of the Northern Song Dynasty]] [[Category:Ancestral Halls]] [[Category:Undesignated Immovable Cultural Relics in Fujian Province]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] }} [[zh:浦西境福寿宫]] [[ja:浦西福寿宮]] [[de:Puxi Fushou Palace]]

|page=118-128 |issn=1000-4289}} and '''Fuzhou Fushou Palace'''{{Cite journal |author=Lin Zizhou |title=A Comparative Study of Manichaean Relics between Xiapu and Fushou Palace in Fuzhou |journal={{ill|Shihezi University|de|Shihezi University|ja|石河子大学|zh|石河子大学|12=draft}} Journal (Philosophy and Social Sciences Edition) |publication-place={{ill|Shihezi City|de|Stadt Shihezi|ja|石河子市|zh|石河子市|12=draft}} |publication-date=2016 |issue=06 |page=33-39 |doi=10.13880/j.cnki.cn65-1210/c.20161221.010 |issn=1671-0304}}, formerly known as '''Mingjiao Wenfó Patriarch Hall''', is a religious building located in the Puxi community of {{ill|Fuzhou|de|Fuzhou|ja|福州市|zh|福州市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Taijiang District|de|Taijiang Bezirk|ja|台江区|zh|台江区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yizhou Subdistrict|de|Yizhou Stadtteil|ja|義洲街道|zh|义洲街道|12=draft}} in {{ill|Fujian Province|de|Fujian Provinz|ja|福建省|zh|福建省|12=draft}}. It is the only remaining {{ill|Manichaeism|de|Manichäismus|ja|摩尼教|zh|摩尼教|12=draft}} building in Fuzhou and is known as a “living fossil of Manichaean culture”. It is currently an immovable cultural relic site in Taijiang District, Fuzhou.{{Cite news|url=http://kejiao.cntv.cn/20120509/100101.shtml|title=Puxi Fushou Palace in Taijiang Confirmed as a Manichaean Temple Ruin|work={{ill|China Network Television|de|China Network Television|ja|中国民間テレビ局|zh|中国网络电视台|12=draft}}|date=2012-05-09|accessdate=2023-10-01|language=zh|archive-date=2021-10-21|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211021213535/http://kejiao.cntv.cn/20120509/100101.shtml|dead-url=no}} Puxi Fushou Palace is also one of the thirteen halls in the faith of {{ill|Jiu'an Taishan|de|Jiu'an Taishan|ja|九案泰山|zh|九案泰山|12=draft}}. == History == [[File:浦西福寿宫内殿.jpg|thumb|Interior Hall of Puxi Fushou Palace]] The Mingjiao Wenfó Patriarch Hall was originally built during the Song Dynasty and has undergone multiple restorations and relocations over the years. According to the records in the {{ill|Wanli|de|Wanli|ja|万曆|zh|万历|12=draft}} edition of the {{ill|Funing Prefecture|de|Funing Präfektur|ja|福寧州|zh|福宁州|12=draft}} Gazetteer, the hall was constructed to commemorate the Manichaean patriarch {{ill|Lin Deng|de|Lin Deng|ja|林瞪|zh|林瞪|12=draft}}, who prayed for rain in {{ill|Min County|de|Min Landkreis|ja|閩縣|zh|闽县|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Jiayou|de|Jiayou|ja|嘉祐|zh|嘉祐|12=draft}} era of the Northern Song. Due to the generally prohibitive attitude toward Mingjiao during Chinese history (especially in the {{ill|Ming Dynasty|de|Ming-Dynasty|ja|明朝|zh|明朝|12=draft}}), local believers of the Xiao clan outwardly converted to {{ill|Taoism|de|Daoismus|ja|道教|zh|道教|12=draft}}, enshrining other Taoist and folk deities to secretly safeguard the deity of Mingjiao Wenfó{{Cite journal |author=Li Linzhou |title=An Important Manichaean Site in Fuzhou — Fushou Palace in Yizhou, Taijiang, Fuzhou |journal=Fujian Religion |publication-date=2004 |issue=01 |page=43-44}}. Until 1997, the original site of the ancestral hall was located at the present-day Fuzhou MSG Factory; in 1997, due to the construction of the factory on that site, it was relocated to its current location in Puxi{{Cite journal |author=Chen Yizhou |author2=Tu Yuanji |title=A Study on the Manichaean Temple Ruins in Fujian |journal=Maritime History Research |publication-place={{ill|Quanzhou|de|Quanzhou|ja|泉州市|zh|泉州市|12=draft}} |publication-date=2004 |issue=01 |page=75-83 |issn=1006-8384}}. Owing to the unclear religious affiliation at the time, in 1998 the authorities renamed Wenfó Patriarch Hall as “Fushou Palace” to grant it a legitimate religious status and incorporated it into Fuzhou’s {{ill|Taoism|de|Daoismus|ja|道教|zh|道教|12=draft}} temple system{{Cite book|title=Fuzhou Annals (1995—2005)|last=Fuzhou Local Annals Compilation Committee|publisher=Fangzhi Press|year=2017|isbn=978-7-5034-9649-3|location=Beijing|pages=826}}; in the early 2000s, due to insufficient evidence, there remained considerable academic debate over its religious affiliation, until after the discovery and study of the {{ill|Xiapu Documents|de|Xiapu Dokumente|ja|霞浦文書|zh|霞浦文书|12=draft}} in 2008 confirmed that Fushou Palace is indeed a {{ill|Manichaeism|de|Manichäismus|ja|摩尼教|zh|摩尼教|12=draft}} ({{ill|Mingjiao|de|Mingjiao|ja|明教|zh|明教|12=draft}}) temple – the best-preserved and most fully equipped of its kind today. The Mingjiao Wenfó Patriarch Hall also has a subsidiary hall located in Shisha Natural Village, {{ill|Shangjie Town|de|Shangjie Town|ja|上街鎮|zh|上街镇|12=draft}}, Minhou County. This temple originated over 200 years ago when local believers’ ancestors branched off from the ancestral hall; the two main deities enshrined here are identical to those of the main hall. == Architecture == The original site of the Mingjiao Wenfó Patriarch Hall (up to 1997) faced east with its entrance oriented westward, in line with the Manichaean concept of venerating the west. It covered an area of 3.5 mu with a building area of 2 mu, comprising a main hall, an auxiliary hall, a performance stage, a banquet viewing platform, a kitchen, and a garden. The current site of Puxi Fushou Palace spans over 900 square meters and faces south. The main building occupies 742.6 square meters, has a depth of 22.6 meters, a main hall width of 14.7 meters, and a height of 10 meters. It primarily enshrines two principal deities: the Mingjiao Wenfó modeled after {{ill|Mani|de|Mani|ja|摩尼|zh|摩尼|12=draft}}, and Dushi Zhenjun modeled after the Fujian Manichaean patriarch {{ill|Lin Deng|de|Lin Deng|ja|林瞪|zh|林瞪|12=draft}}. In the main hall, flanking the principal deity on its left and right are the auxiliary deities {{ill|Zhang Zhenjun|de|Zhang Zhenjun|ja|張真君|zh|张真君|12=draft}} and {{ill|Zhenwu Dadi|de|Zhenwu Dadi|ja|真武大帝|zh|真武大帝|12=draft}}, respectively, along with thirty-six guardian deities. In addition, Fushou Palace has three subsidiary halls – the {{ill|Linshui Furen Hall|de|Linshui Furen Hall|ja|臨水夫人殿|zh|临水夫人殿|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Huaguang Hall|de|Huaguang Hall|ja|华光殿|zh|华光殿|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|Jingzhu|de|Jingzhu|ja|境主|zh|境主|lt=Dawang|lt_ja=大王|lt_de=Dawang|lt_zh=大王|12=draft}} Hall – which enshrine {{ill|Linshui Furen|de|Linshui Furen|ja|臨水夫人|zh|臨水夫人|12=draft}}, {{ill|Huaguang Dadi|de|Huaguang Dadi|ja|华光大帝|zh|华光大帝|12=draft}} and the two kings, Huang and Zhao, respectively. == Deity System == The deities enshrined within Fushou Palace comprise figures from Manichaeism, {{ill|Taoism|de|Daoismus|ja|道教|zh|道教|12=draft}}, {{ill|Buddhism|de|Buddhismus|ja|佛教|zh|佛教|12=draft}}, and local {{ill|Chinese Folk Religion|de|Chinesischer Volksglaube|ja|中国民間信仰|zh|中国民间信仰|lt=Folk Religion|lt_ja=民間信仰|lt_de=Volksglaube|lt_zh=民间信仰|12=draft}}, all merged within one palace. It is also the only Manichaean temple with a complete configuration and continuously maintained incense traditions to this day. === Main Deities === The principal deities of Fushou Palace are Mingjiao Wenfó and Dushi Zhenren (also known as Dushi Zhenjun), representing respectively the primary deity of Manichaeism, {{ill|Mani|de|Mani|ja|摩尼|zh|摩尼|12=draft}} (216–270 AD), and the Xiapu Manichaean leader {{ill|Lin Deng|de|Lin Deng|ja|林瞪|zh|林瞪|12=draft}} (1003–1059 AD). {{multiple image | align = right | total_width = 260 | image1 = 浦西境福寿宫中的明教文佛.jpg | width1 = 300 | height1 = 350 | caption1 = Mingjiao Wenfó | image2 = 浦西境福寿宫中的度师真君.jpg | width2 = 300 | height2 = 350 | caption2 = Dushi Zhenjun | caption_align = center }} In the earliest studies, scholars such as {{ill|Li Linzhou|de|Li Linzhou|ja|李林洲|zh|李林洲|12=draft}} conducted field research and, equating Mingjiao with Manichaeism – citing the presence of “Wenfó” couplets in the modest lodgings of Manichaean temples – argued that the Mingjiao Wenfó in Fushou Palace was in fact the chief deity Mani Buddha of Manichaeism, with Dushi Zhenjun representing a certain Manichaean master. However, due to insufficient evidence, this view was challenged in 2004 by Manichaean scholars such as {{ill|Lin Wushu|de|Lin Wushu|ja|林悟殊|zh|林悟殊|12=draft}}. Between 2008 and 2009, with the discovery of the {{ill|Xiapu Documents|de|Xiapu Dokumente|ja|霞浦文書|zh|霞浦文书|12=draft}}, a clearer understanding of the two main deities of the Mingjiao Wenfó Patriarch Hall emerged{{Cite news|title=Fuzhou Fushou Palace Has a Close Relationship with Mingjiao|author=Wen Hailong|work=Fuzhou Daily|date=2009-11-17|language=zh}}. The patterns and adornments on Mingjiao Wenfó, as well as many scenes depicted in the murals behind him – notably the sunburst on his head – resemble those found in Xiapu Manichaean artworks (a decorative element absent in other Taoist temples in Fuzhou), thereby confirming his affiliation with the Xiapu Manichaean system. At present, academic opinions on the identity of Mingjiao Wenfó vary; aside from the prevailing view that he is the Manichaean chief deity Mani, some scholars suggest that he may be a “Wenfó envoy” or related to what the Xiapu documents refer to as {{ill|Shakyamuni Wenfó|de|Shakyamuni Wenfó|ja|釈迦文佛|zh|釋迦文佛|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sizhou Great Saint|de|Sizhou Großer Heiliger|ja|泗州大聖|zh|泗州大聖|lt=Sizhou Wenfó|lt_ja=泗州文佛|lt_de=Sizhou Wenfó|lt_zh=泗州文佛|12=draft}}.{{Cite journal |author=Yang Fuxue |author2=Li Xiaoyan |author3=Peng Xiaojing |title=Major Findings from the Survey of Manichaean Relics in Fujian |journal=Studies in Religious Research |publication-place={{ill|Chengdu|de|Chengdu|ja|成都市|zh|成都市|12=draft}} |publication-date=2017 |issue=04 |page=259-271 |issn=1006-1312}} === Ancillary Deities in the Main Hall === {{Multiple image | image1 = 浦西福寿宫张圣真君.jpg | caption1 = Zhang Zhenjun | image2 = 浦西福寿宫玄天上帝.jpg | caption2 = Xuántiān Shangdi | perrow = 2/2 | align = right | total_width = 300 }} The ancestral hall enshrines, on the west and east sides of its north wall (the side where the principal deity is located), the deities {{ill|Zhang Zhenjun|de|Zhang Zhenjun|ja|張真君|zh|张圣真君|12=draft}} and {{ill|Xuántiān Shangdi|de|Xuántiān Shangdi|ja|玄天上帝|zh|玄天上帝|12=draft}}, both figures associated with Taoism. {{NoteTag|Zhang Zhenjun (also known as Zhang Zhenjun or Zhang Shengjun) is the chief of the Thunder Regulation of the Thirty-Three Heavens, born in {{ill|Yongtai County|de|Yongtai County|ja|永泰县|zh|永泰县|12=draft}}, and a direct disciple of {{ill|Xu Zhenjun|de|Xu Zhenjun|ja|許真君|zh|许真君|12=draft}} of the Lushan Sect. Local tradition holds that Lin Deng once studied the Daoist techniques transmitted by Xu Zhenjun on Lushan. Additionally, the Xiapu Documents mention information related to the Lushan Sect. Another reason for enshrining Zhang Zhenjun may be that Lin Deng was conferred the title “Dongtian Dulei Shi” (as rendered in various sources) in recognition of his merit in praying for rain.}} According to local tradition, Zhang Zhenjun is the Thunder Regulation Chief; indeed, Fuzhou still preserves the {{ill|Zhang Zhenjun Ancestral Hall|de|Zhang Zhenjun Ancestral Hall|ja|張聖真君祖殿|zh|张圣真君祖殿|12=draft}} in {{ill|Minqing County|de|Minqing County|ja|闽清县|zh|闽清县|12=draft}}{{Cite web|date=2023-06-27|title=Physical Geography|url=http://www.fzmq.gov.cn/xjwz/zjmq/zrdl/201704/t20170413_391146.htm|access-date=2023-10-30|website=Minqing County Government|archive-date=2022-11-20|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221120015517/http://www.fzmq.gov.cn/xjwz/zjmq/zrdl/201704/t20170413_391146.htm|dead-url=no}} and the Fujian Provincial Cultural Heritage Unit {{ill|Zhang Zhenjun Ancestral Hall|de|Zhang Zhenjun Ancestral Hall|ja|張真君祖殿|zh|张真君祖殿|12=draft}} (located in {{ill|Taijiang District|de|Taijiang Bezirk|ja|台江区|zh|台江区|12=draft}}). Xuántiān Shangdi, as recorded in the Xiapu Documents, is referred to as “Northern Guardian Zhenwu Bodhisattva”{{NoteTag|Term derived from the title conferred by Emperor Yongle: “Northern Polar Guardian Zhenwu Xuántiān Shangdi”}} or “Great Saint Zhenwu”. He is a Taoist figure of the {{ill|Xuanwu Sect|de|Xuanwu Sect|ja|玄武派|zh|玄武派|12=draft}}, regarded as the 82nd transformation of the Supreme Elderly Lord and one of Taoism’s four major guardian deities (the others being the Azure Dragon, White Tiger, and Vermilion Bird). In most Taoist temples, figures from the Lushan and Xuanwu sects do not coexist; however, Fushou Palace enshrines deities from both, reflecting its unique position as a Manichaean temple that both draws on and distinguishes itself from Taoism. === Three Halls and One Pavilion === Fushou Palace features four subsidiary halls: on the left is the {{ill|Linshui Furen Hall|de|Linshui Furen Hall|ja|臨水夫人殿|zh|临水夫人殿|12=draft}}, while on the right are the {{ill|Guanyin Pavilion|de|Guanyin Pavillon|ja|観音閣|zh|观音阁|12=draft}}, the hall of {{ill|Huaguang Dadi|de|Huaguang Dadi|ja|华光大帝|zh|华光大帝|lt=Huaguang|lt_ja=华光|lt_de=Huaguang|lt_zh=华光|12=draft}}, and the hall of {{ill|Jingzhu|de|Jingzhu|ja|境主|zh|境主|lt=Dawang|lt_ja=大王|lt_de=Dawang|lt_zh=Dawang|12=draft}}. According to inscriptions on old stone tablets preserved within the palace, adjacent to the original site of Fushou Palace were the Yulin Palace (dedicated to Linshui Furen) and Dawang Hall, while the Guanyin Pavilion and Huaguang Hall were later additions. The deities in these halls belong respectively to the {{ill|Buddhism|de|Buddhismus|ja|佛教|zh|佛教|12=draft}} and Taoist systems. == Notes == {{NoteFoot}} == See Also == {{Commons category}} {{fzcuo|1742|Puxi Fushou Palace}} * {{ill|Leshantang|de|Leshantang|ja|乐山堂|zh|乐山堂|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tahou Third Buddha Pagoda|de|Tahou Third Buddha Pagoda|ja|塔后三佛塔|zh|塔后三佛塔|12=draft}} * {{ill|Feilu Pagoda|de|Feilu Pagoda|ja|飞路塔|zh|飞路塔|12=draft}} * {{ill|Cao'an|de|Cao'an|ja|草庵|zh|草庵|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Manichaeism footer}} {{Manichaeism}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buildings in Taijiang District]] [[Category:Taoist Buildings in Fuzhou]] [[Category:Manichaean Buildings]] [[Category:Religious Buildings of the Northern Song Dynasty]] [[Category:Ancestral Halls]] [[Category:Undesignated Immovable Cultural Relics in Fujian Province]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] }} [[zh:浦西境福寿宫]] [[ja:浦西福寿宮]] [[de:Puxi Fushou Palace]]

{{ill|Taoism|de|Daoismus|ja|道教|zh|道教|12=draft}}、{{ill|Chinese Folk Religion|de|Chinesischer Volksglaube|ja|中国民間信仰|zh|中国民间信仰|lt=Fujian Folk Religion|lt_ja=福建民間信仰|lt_de=Fujian Volksglaube|lt_zh=福建民间信仰|12=draft}} | heritage = {{ill|Taijiang District|de|Taijiang Bezirk|ja|台江区|zh|台江区|12=draft}} Immovable Cultural Relic | architecture_type =
