[[cdo:Puō-să̤ Hók-sêu-gṳ̆ng]][[commons:Category:Puxi Fushou Temple]][[zh:浦西境福寿宫]] [[cdo:Puō-să̤ Hók-sêu-gṳ̆ng]][[commons:Category:Puxi Fushou Temple]][[zh:浦西境福寿宫]] [[cdo:Puō-să̤ Hók-sêu-gṳ̆ng]][[sn:commons:Category:Puxi Fushou Temple]][[zh:浦西境福寿宫]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: [[da:d:Q122897824]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:25, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | building_name = Puxi Fushou Temple
（Mingjiao Wenfó Patriarch Hall） | image = 浦西福寿宫外景 03.jpg | caption = Exterior view of Puxi Fushou Temple | coordinates = {{coord|26|3|46.76|N|119|17|23.82|E|scale:10000|display=title,inline}} | mapframe-zoom = 13 | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Manichaeism|Manichaeism]]（[[:en:Manichaeism|Manichaeism]]）
[[:en:Taoism|Taoism]]、[[:en:Chinese folk religion|Fujian Folk Religion]] | heritage = [[:en:Taijiang, Fuzhou|Taijiang District]] Immovable Cultural Relic | architecture_type = [[:en:Temple|Temple]] | totalarea = Approximately 900㎡ | location = {{PRC}}[[:en:Fujian|Fujian Province]][[:en:Fuzhou|Fuzhou]][[:en:Taijiang, Fuzhou|Taijiang District]]{{ill|Yizhou Subdistrict|de|Yizhou Stadtteil|ja|義洲街道|zh|义洲街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yizhou Subdistrict|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Puxi Village 6-1 }} '''Fushou Palace'''{{Cite journal |author=Lin Wushu |title=An Analysis of the Religious Attributes of “Mingjiao Wenfó” at Puxi Fushou Temple in Fuzhou |journal=Journal of Sun Yat-sen University (Social Sciences Edition) |publication-date=2004 |issue=06 |page=118-123 |issn=1000-9639}}, also known as '''Puxi Region Fushou Palace'''{{Cite journal |author=Peng Xiaojing |author2=Yang Fuxue |title=An Investigation into the Manichaean Deities at Fushou Palace in Fuzhou |journal=World Religious Studies |publication-date=2016 |issue=03 |page=118-128 |issn=1000-4289}} and '''Fuzhou Fushou Palace'''{{Cite journal |author=Lin Zizhou |title=A Comparative Study of Manichaean Relics between Xiapu and Fushou Palace in Fuzhou |journal={{ill|Shihezi University|de|Shihezi University|ja|石河子大学|zh|石河子大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shihezi University|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Journal (Philosophy and Social Sciences Edition) |publication-place=[[:en:Shihezi|Shihezi City]] |publication-date=2016 |issue=06 |page=33-39 |doi=10.13880/j.cnki.cn65-1210/c.20161221.010 |issn=1671-0304}}, formerly known as '''Mingjiao Wenfó Patriarch Hall''', is a religious building located in the Puxi community of [[:en:Fuzhou|Fuzhou]], [[:en:Taijiang, Fuzhou|Taijiang District]], {{ill|Yizhou Subdistrict|de|Yizhou Stadtteil|ja|義洲街道|zh|义洲街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yizhou Subdistrict|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Fujian|Fujian Province]]. It is the only remaining [[:en:Manichaeism|Manichaean]] building in Fuzhou and is known as a “living fossil of Manichaean culture”. It is currently an immovable cultural relic site in Taijiang District, Fuzhou.{{Cite news|url=http://kejiao.cntv.cn/20120509/100101.shtml|title=Puxi Fushou Temple in Taijiang Confirmed as a Manichaean Temple Ruin|work={{ill|China Network Television|de|China Network Television|ja|中国民間テレビ局|zh|中国网络电视台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China Network Television|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|date=2012-05-09|accessdate=2023-10-01|language=zh|archive-date=2021-10-21|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211021213535/http://kejiao.cntv.cn/20120509/100101.shtml|dead-url=no}} Puxi Fushou Temple is also one of the thirteen halls in the faith of {{ill|Jiu'an Taishan|de|Jiu'an Taishan|ja|九案泰山|zh|九案泰山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jiu'an Taishan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == History == [[File:浦西福寿宫内殿.jpg|thumb|Interior Hall of Puxi Fushou Temple]] The Mingjiao Wenfó Patriarch Hall was originally built during the Song Dynasty and has undergone multiple restorations and relocations over the years. According to the records in the [[:en:Wanli Emperor|Wanli]] edition of the {{ill|Funing Prefecture|de|Funing Präfektur|ja|福寧州|zh|福宁州|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funing Prefecture|1=Funing Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16078627}} Gazetteer, the hall was constructed to commemorate the Manichaean patriarch {{ill|Lin Deng|de|Lin Deng|ja|林瞪|zh|林瞪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lin Deng|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who prayed for rain in {{ill|Min County|de|Min Landkreis|ja|閩縣|zh|闽县|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Min County|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the {{ill|Jiayou|de|Jiayou|ja|嘉祐|zh|嘉祐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jiayou|1=Jiayou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10924055}} era of the Northern Song. Due to the generally prohibitive attitude toward Mingjiao during Chinese history (especially in the {{ill|Ming Dynasty|de|Ming-Dynasty|ja|明朝|zh|明朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ming Dynasty|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明|1=Ming dynasty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9903}}), local believers of the Xiao clan outwardly converted to [[:en:Taoism|Taoism]], enshrining other Taoist and folk deities to secretly safeguard the deity of Mingjiao Wenfó{{Cite journal |author=Li Linzhou |title=An Important Manichaean Site in Fuzhou — Fushou Palace in Yizhou, Taijiang, Fuzhou |journal=Fujian Religion |publication-date=2004 |issue=01 |page=43-44}}. Until 1997, the original site of the ancestral hall was located at the present-day Fuzhou MSG Factory; in 1997, due to the construction of the factory on that site, it was relocated to its current location in Puxi{{Cite journal |author=Chen Yizhou |author2=Tu Yuanji |title=A Study on the Manichaean Temple Ruins in Fujian |journal=Maritime History Research |publication-place=[[:en:Quanzhou|Quanzhou]] |publication-date=2004 |issue=01 |page=75-83 |issn=1006-8384}}. Owing to the unclear religious affiliation at the time, in 1998 the authorities renamed Wenfó Patriarch Hall as “Fushou Palace” to grant it a legitimate religious status and incorporated it into Fuzhou’s [[:en:Taoism|Taoism]] temple system{{Cite book|title=Fuzhou Annals (1995—2005)|last=Fuzhou Local Annals Compilation Committee|publisher=Fangzhi Press|year=2017|isbn=978-7-5034-9649-3|location=Beijing|pages=826}}; in the early 2000s, due to insufficient evidence, there remained considerable academic debate over its religious affiliation, until after the discovery and study of the {{ill|Xiapu Documents|de|Xiapu Dokumente|ja|霞浦文書|zh|霞浦文书|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xiapu Documents|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 2008 confirmed that Fushou Palace is indeed a [[:en:Manichaeism|Manichaeism]] ({{ill|Mingjiao|de|Mingjiao|ja|明教|zh|明教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mingjiao|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 唐代三夷教|1=Three Persian religions in Tang dynasty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418516}}) temple – the best-preserved and most fully equipped of its kind today. The Mingjiao Wenfó Patriarch Hall also has a subsidiary hall located in Shisha Natural Village, {{ill|Shangjie Town|de|Shangjie Town|ja|上街鎮|zh|上街镇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shangjie Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Minhou County. This temple originated over 200 years ago when local believers’ ancestors branched off from the ancestral hall; the two main deities enshrined here are identical to those of the main hall. == Architecture == The original site of the Mingjiao Wenfó Patriarch Hall (up to 1997) faced east with its entrance oriented westward, in line with the Manichaean concept of venerating the west. It covered an area of 3.5 mu with a building area of 2 mu, comprising a main hall, an auxiliary hall, a performance stage, a banquet viewing platform, a kitchen, and a garden. The current site of Puxi Fushou Temple spans over 900 square meters and faces south. The main building occupies 742.6 square meters, has a depth of 22.6 meters, a main hall width of 14.7 meters, and a height of 10 meters. It primarily enshrines two principal deities: the Mingjiao Wenfó modeled after [[:en:Mani|Mani]], and Dushi Zhenjun modeled after the Fujian Manichaean patriarch {{ill|Lin Deng|de|Lin Deng|ja|林瞪|zh|林瞪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lin Deng|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the main hall, flanking the principal deity on its left and right are the auxiliary deities {{ill|Zhang Zhenjun|de|Zhang Zhenjun|ja|張真君|zh|张真君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhang Zhenjun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Xuanwu (god)|de|Zhenwu Dadi|ja|真武大帝|zh|真武大帝|lt=Zhenwu Dadi|Xuanwu (god)=Xuanwu (god)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xuanwu (god)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, respectively, along with thirty-six guardian deities. In addition, Fushou Palace has three subsidiary halls – the {{ill|Linshui Furen Hall|de|Linshui Furen Hall|ja|臨水夫人殿|zh|临水夫人殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Linshui Furen Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the {{ill|Huaguang Hall|de|Huaguang Hall|ja|华光殿|zh|华光殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Huaguang Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the {{ill|Jingzhu|de|Jingzhu|ja|境主|zh|境主|lt=Dawang|lt_ja=大王|lt_de=Dawang|lt_zh=大王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingzhu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Hall – which enshrine {{ill|Linshui Furen|de|Linshui Furen|ja|臨水夫人|zh|臨水夫人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Linshui Furen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Huaguang Dadi|de|Huaguang Dadi|ja|华光大帝|zh|华光大帝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Huaguang Dadi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the two kings, Huang and Zhao, respectively. == Deity System == The deities enshrined within Fushou Palace comprise figures from Manichaeism, [[:en:Taoism|Taoism]], [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], and local [[:en:Chinese folk religion|Folk Religion]], all merged within one palace. It is also the only Manichaean temple with a complete configuration and continuously maintained incense traditions to this day. === Main Deities === The principal deities of Fushou Palace are Mingjiao Wenfó and Dushi Zhenren (also known as Dushi Zhenjun), representing respectively the primary deity of Manichaeism, [[:en:Mani|Mani]] (216–270 AD), and the Xiapu Manichaean leader {{ill|Lin Deng|de|Lin Deng|ja|林瞪|zh|林瞪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lin Deng|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1003–1059 AD). {{multiple image | align = right | total_width = 260 | image1 = 浦西境福寿宫中的明教文佛.jpg | width1 = 300 | height1 = 350 | caption1 = Mingjiao Wenfó | image2 = 浦西境福寿宫中的度师真君.jpg | width2 = 300 | height2 = 350 | caption2 = Dushi Zhenjun | caption_align = center }} In the earliest studies, scholars such as {{ill|Li Linzhou|de|Li Linzhou|ja|李林洲|zh|李林洲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Li Linzhou|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} conducted field research and, equating Mingjiao with Manichaeism – citing the presence of “Wenfó” couplets in the modest lodgings of Manichaean temples – argued that the Mingjiao Wenfó in Fushou Palace was in fact the chief deity Mani Buddha of Manichaeism, with Dushi Zhenjun representing a certain Manichaean master. However, due to insufficient evidence, this view was challenged in 2004 by Manichaean scholars such as {{ill|Lin Wushu|de|Lin Wushu|ja|林悟殊|zh|林悟殊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lin Wushu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Between 2008 and 2009, with the discovery of the {{ill|Xiapu Documents|de|Xiapu Dokumente|ja|霞浦文書|zh|霞浦文书|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xiapu Documents|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a clearer understanding of the two main deities of the Mingjiao Wenfó Patriarch Hall emerged{{Cite news|title=Fuzhou Fushou Palace Has a Close Relationship with Mingjiao|author=Wen Hailong|work=Fuzhou Daily|date=2009-11-17|language=zh}}. The patterns and adornments on Mingjiao Wenfó, as well as many scenes depicted in the murals behind him – notably the sunburst on his head – resemble those found in Xiapu Manichaean artworks (a decorative element absent in other Taoist temples in Fuzhou), thereby confirming his affiliation with the Xiapu Manichaean system. At present, academic opinions on the identity of Mingjiao Wenfó vary; aside from the prevailing view that he is the Manichaean chief deity Mani, some scholars suggest that he may be a “Wenfó envoy” or related to what the Xiapu documents refer to as {{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata Wenfó|de|Shakyamuni Tathāgata Wenfó|ja|釈迦文佛|zh|釋迦文佛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata Wenfó|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Sizhou Great Saint|de|Sizhou Großer Heiliger|ja|泗州大聖|zh|泗州大聖|lt=Sizhou Wenfó|lt_ja=泗州文佛|lt_de=Sizhou Wenfó|lt_zh=泗州文佛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sizhou Great Saint|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Cite journal |author=Yang Fuxue |author2=Li Xiaoyan |author3=Peng Xiaojing |title=Major Findings from the Survey of Manichaean Relics in Fujian |journal=Studies in Religious Research |publication-place=[[:en:Chengdu|Chengdu]] |publication-date=2017 |issue=04 |page=259-271 |issn=1006-1312}} === Ancillary Deities in the Main Hall === {{Multiple image | image1 = 浦西福寿宫张圣真君.jpg | caption1 = Zhang Zhenjun | image2 = 浦西福寿宫玄天上帝.jpg | caption2 = Xuántiān Shangdi | perrow = 2/2 | align = right | total_width = 300 }} The ancestral hall enshrines, on the west and east sides of its north wall (the side where the principal deity is located), the deities {{ill|Zhang Zhenjun|de|Zhang Zhenjun|ja|張真君|zh|张圣真君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhang Zhenjun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Xuanwu (god)|Xuántiān Shangdi]], both figures associated with Taoism. {{NoteTag|Zhang Zhenjun (also known as Zhang Zhenjun or Zhang Shengjun) is the chief of the Thunder Regulation of the Thirty-Three Heavens, born in {{ill|Yongtai County|de|Yongtai County|ja|永泰县|zh|永泰县|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yongtai County|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a direct disciple of {{ill|Xu Zhenjun|de|Xu Zhenjun|ja|許真君|zh|许真君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xu Zhenjun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Lushan Sect. Local tradition holds that Lin Deng once studied the Daoist techniques transmitted by Xu Zhenjun on Lushan. Additionally, the Xiapu Documents mention information related to the Lushan Sect. Another reason for enshrining Zhang Zhenjun may be that Lin Deng was conferred the title “Dongtian Dulei Shi” (as rendered in various sources) in recognition of his merit in praying for rain.}} According to local tradition, Zhang Zhenjun is the Thunder Regulation Chief; indeed, Fuzhou still preserves the {{ill|Zhang Zhenjun Ancestral Hall|de|Zhang Zhenjun Ancestral Hall|ja|張聖真君祖殿|zh|张圣真君祖殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhang Zhenjun Ancestral Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Minqing County|de|Minqing County|ja|闽清县|zh|闽清县|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minqing County|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Cite web|date=2023-06-27|title=Physical Geography|url=http://www.fzmq.gov.cn/xjwz/zjmq/zrdl/201704/t20170413_391146.htm|access-date=2023-10-30|website=Minqing County Government|archive-date=2022-11-20|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221120015517/http://www.fzmq.gov.cn/xjwz/zjmq/zrdl/201704/t20170413_391146.htm|dead-url=no}} and the Fujian Provincial Cultural Heritage Unit {{ill|Zhang Zhenjun Ancestral Hall|de|Zhang Zhenjun Ancestral Hall|ja|張真君祖殿|zh|张真君祖殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhang Zhenjun Ancestral Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (located in [[:en:Taijiang, Fuzhou|Taijiang District]]). Xuántiān Shangdi, as recorded in the Xiapu Documents, is referred to as “Northern Guardian Zhenwu Bodhisattva”{{NoteTag|Term derived from the title conferred by Emperor Yongle: “Northern Polar Guardian Zhenwu Xuántiān Shangdi”}} or “Great Saint Zhenwu”. He is a Taoist figure of the {{ill|Xuanwu Sect|de|Xuanwu Sect|ja|玄武派|zh|玄武派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xuanwu Sect|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, regarded as the 82nd transformation of the Supreme Elderly Lord and one of Taoism’s four major guardian deities (the others being the Azure Dragon, White Tiger, and Vermilion Bird). In most Taoist temples, figures from the Lushan and Xuanwu sects do not coexist; however, Fushou Palace enshrines deities from both, reflecting its unique position as a Manichaean temple that both draws on and distinguishes itself from Taoism. === Three Halls and One Pavilion === Fushou Palace features four subsidiary halls: on the left is the {{ill|Linshui Furen Hall|de|Linshui Furen Hall|ja|臨水夫人殿|zh|临水夫人殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Linshui Furen Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while on the right are the {{ill|Guanyin Pavilion|de|Guanyin Pavillon|ja|観音閣|zh|观音阁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guanyin Pavilion|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the hall of {{ill|Huaguang Dadi|de|Huaguang Dadi|ja|华光大帝|zh|华光大帝|lt=Huaguang|lt_ja=华光|lt_de=Huaguang|lt_zh=华光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Huaguang Dadi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the hall of {{ill|Jingzhu|de|Jingzhu|ja|境主|zh|境主|lt=Dawang|lt_ja=大王|lt_de=Dawang|lt_zh=Dawang|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingzhu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. According to inscriptions on old stone tablets preserved within the palace, adjacent to the original site of Fushou Palace were the Yulin Palace (dedicated to Linshui Furen) and Dawang Hall, while the Guanyin Pavilion and Huaguang Hall were later additions. The deities in these halls belong respectively to the [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] and Taoist systems. == Notes == {{NoteFoot}} == See Also == {{Commons category}} {{fzcuo|1742|Puxi Fushou Temple}} * {{ill|Leshantang|de|Leshantang|ja|乐山堂|zh|乐山堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Leshantang|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Tahou Third Buddha Pagoda|de|Tahou Third Buddha Pagoda|ja|塔后三佛塔|zh|塔后三佛塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahou Third Buddha Pagoda|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Feilu Pagoda|de|Feilu Pagoda|ja|飞路塔|zh|飞路塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feilu Pagoda|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Cao'an|de|Cao'an|ja|草庵|zh|草庵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cao'an|1=sōan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24897528}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Manichaeism footer}} {{Manichaeism}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:浦西境福寿宫]] [[zh:浦西境福寿宫]] [[ja:浦西福寿宮]] [[de:Puxi Fushou Temple]] [[cdo:浦西福壽宮]] {{Commons category|Puxi Fushou Temple}} {{translated page|zh|浦西境福寿宫|version=86359806|comment=Imported full zh history}} {{wikidata link|Q122897824}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:de import failed]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[cdo:Puō-să̤ Hók-sêu-gṳ̆ng]][[commons:Category:Puxi Fushou Temple]][[zh:浦西境福寿宫]] [[cdo:Puō-să̤ Hók-sêu-gṳ̆ng]][[commons:Category:Puxi Fushou Temple]][[zh:浦西境福寿宫]] [[cdo:Puō-să̤ Hók-sêu-gṳ̆ng]][[sn:commons:Category:Puxi Fushou Temple]][[zh:浦西境福寿宫]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: [[da:d:Q122897824]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:25, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | building_name = Puxi Fushou Temple
（Mingjiao Wenfó Patriarch Hall） | image = 浦西福寿宫外景 03.jpg | caption = Exterior view of Puxi Fushou Temple | coordinates = {{coord|26|3|46.76|N|119|17|23.82|E|scale:10000|display=title,inline}} | mapframe-zoom = 13 | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Manichaeism|Manichaeism]]（[[:en:Manichaeism|Manichaeism]]）
[[:en:Taoism|Taoism]]、[[:en:Chinese folk religion|Fujian Folk Religion]] | heritage = [[:en:Taijiang, Fuzhou|Taijiang District]] Immovable Cultural Relic | architecture_type = [[:en:Temple|Temple]] | totalarea = Approximately 900㎡ | location = {{PRC}}[[:en:Fujian|Fujian Province]][[:en:Fuzhou|Fuzhou]][[:en:Taijiang, Fuzhou|Taijiang District]]{{ill|Yizhou Subdistrict|de|Yizhou Stadtteil|ja|義洲街道|zh|义洲街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yizhou Subdistrict|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Puxi Village 6-1 }} '''Fushou Palace'''{{Cite journal |author=Lin Wushu |title=An Analysis of the Religious Attributes of “Mingjiao Wenfó” at Puxi Fushou Temple in Fuzhou |journal=Journal of Sun Yat-sen University (Social Sciences Edition) |publication-date=2004 |issue=06 |page=118-123 |issn=1000-9639}}, also known as '''Puxi Region Fushou Palace'''{{Cite journal |author=Peng Xiaojing |author2=Yang Fuxue |title=An Investigation into the Manichaean Deities at Fushou Palace in Fuzhou |journal=World Religious Studies |publication-date=2016 |issue=03 |page=118-128 |issn=1000-4289}} and '''Fuzhou Fushou Palace'''{{Cite journal |author=Lin Zizhou |title=A Comparative Study of Manichaean Relics between Xiapu and Fushou Palace in Fuzhou |journal={{ill|Shihezi University|de|Shihezi University|ja|石河子大学|zh|石河子大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shihezi University|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Journal (Philosophy and Social Sciences Edition) |publication-place=[[:en:Shihezi|Shihezi City]] |publication-date=2016 |issue=06 |page=33-39 |doi=10.13880/j.cnki.cn65-1210/c.20161221.010 |issn=1671-0304}}, formerly known as '''Mingjiao Wenfó Patriarch Hall''', is a religious building located in the Puxi community of [[:en:Fuzhou|Fuzhou]], [[:en:Taijiang, Fuzhou|Taijiang District]], {{ill|Yizhou Subdistrict|de|Yizhou Stadtteil|ja|義洲街道|zh|义洲街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yizhou Subdistrict|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Fujian|Fujian Province]]. It is the only remaining [[:en:Manichaeism|Manichaean]] building in Fuzhou and is known as a “living fossil of Manichaean culture”. It is currently an immovable cultural relic site in Taijiang District, Fuzhou.{{Cite news|url=http://kejiao.cntv.cn/20120509/100101.shtml|title=Puxi Fushou Temple in Taijiang Confirmed as a Manichaean Temple Ruin|work={{ill|China Network Television|de|China Network Television|ja|中国民間テレビ局|zh|中国网络电视台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China Network Television|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|date=2012-05-09|accessdate=2023-10-01|language=zh|archive-date=2021-10-21|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211021213535/http://kejiao.cntv.cn/20120509/100101.shtml|dead-url=no}} Puxi Fushou Temple is also one of the thirteen halls in the faith of {{ill|Jiu'an Taishan|de|Jiu'an Taishan|ja|九案泰山|zh|九案泰山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jiu'an Taishan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == History == [[File:浦西福寿宫内殿.jpg|thumb|Interior Hall of Puxi Fushou Temple]] The Mingjiao Wenfó Patriarch Hall was originally built during the Song Dynasty and has undergone multiple restorations and relocations over the years. According to the records in the [[:en:Wanli Emperor|Wanli]] edition of the {{ill|Funing Prefecture|de|Funing Präfektur|ja|福寧州|zh|福宁州|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funing Prefecture|1=Funing Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16078627}} Gazetteer, the hall was constructed to commemorate the Manichaean patriarch {{ill|Lin Deng|de|Lin Deng|ja|林瞪|zh|林瞪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lin Deng|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who prayed for rain in {{ill|Min County|de|Min Landkreis|ja|閩縣|zh|闽县|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Min County|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the {{ill|Jiayou|de|Jiayou|ja|嘉祐|zh|嘉祐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jiayou|1=Jiayou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10924055}} era of the Northern Song. Due to the generally prohibitive attitude toward Mingjiao during Chinese history (especially in the {{ill|Ming Dynasty|de|Ming-Dynasty|ja|明朝|zh|明朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ming Dynasty|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明|1=Ming dynasty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9903}}), local believers of the Xiao clan outwardly converted to [[:en:Taoism|Taoism]], enshrining other Taoist and folk deities to secretly safeguard the deity of Mingjiao Wenfó{{Cite journal |author=Li Linzhou |title=An Important Manichaean Site in Fuzhou — Fushou Palace in Yizhou, Taijiang, Fuzhou |journal=Fujian Religion |publication-date=2004 |issue=01 |page=43-44}}. Until 1997, the original site of the ancestral hall was located at the present-day Fuzhou MSG Factory; in 1997, due to the construction of the factory on that site, it was relocated to its current location in Puxi{{Cite journal |author=Chen Yizhou |author2=Tu Yuanji |title=A Study on the Manichaean Temple Ruins in Fujian |journal=Maritime History Research |publication-place=[[:en:Quanzhou|Quanzhou]] |publication-date=2004 |issue=01 |page=75-83 |issn=1006-8384}}. Owing to the unclear religious affiliation at the time, in 1998 the authorities renamed Wenfó Patriarch Hall as “Fushou Palace” to grant it a legitimate religious status and incorporated it into Fuzhou’s [[:en:Taoism|Taoism]] temple system{{Cite book|title=Fuzhou Annals (1995—2005)|last=Fuzhou Local Annals Compilation Committee|publisher=Fangzhi Press|year=2017|isbn=978-7-5034-9649-3|location=Beijing|pages=826}}; in the early 2000s, due to insufficient evidence, there remained considerable academic debate over its religious affiliation, until after the discovery and study of the {{ill|Xiapu Documents|de|Xiapu Dokumente|ja|霞浦文書|zh|霞浦文书|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xiapu Documents|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 2008 confirmed that Fushou Palace is indeed a [[:en:Manichaeism|Manichaeism]] ({{ill|Mingjiao|de|Mingjiao|ja|明教|zh|明教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mingjiao|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 唐代三夷教|1=Three Persian religions in Tang dynasty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418516}}) temple – the best-preserved and most fully equipped of its kind today. The Mingjiao Wenfó Patriarch Hall also has a subsidiary hall located in Shisha Natural Village, {{ill|Shangjie Town|de|Shangjie Town|ja|上街鎮|zh|上街镇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shangjie Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Minhou County. This temple originated over 200 years ago when local believers’ ancestors branched off from the ancestral hall; the two main deities enshrined here are identical to those of the main hall. == Architecture == The original site of the Mingjiao Wenfó Patriarch Hall (up to 1997) faced east with its entrance oriented westward, in line with the Manichaean concept of venerating the west. It covered an area of 3.5 mu with a building area of 2 mu, comprising a main hall, an auxiliary hall, a performance stage, a banquet viewing platform, a kitchen, and a garden. The current site of Puxi Fushou Temple spans over 900 square meters and faces south. The main building occupies 742.6 square meters, has a depth of 22.6 meters, a main hall width of 14.7 meters, and a height of 10 meters. It primarily enshrines two principal deities: the Mingjiao Wenfó modeled after [[:en:Mani|Mani]], and Dushi Zhenjun modeled after the Fujian Manichaean patriarch {{ill|Lin Deng|de|Lin Deng|ja|林瞪|zh|林瞪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lin Deng|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the main hall, flanking the principal deity on its left and right are the auxiliary deities {{ill|Zhang Zhenjun|de|Zhang Zhenjun|ja|張真君|zh|张真君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhang Zhenjun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Xuanwu (god)|de|Zhenwu Dadi|ja|真武大帝|zh|真武大帝|lt=Zhenwu Dadi|Xuanwu (god)=Xuanwu (god)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xuanwu (god)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, respectively, along with thirty-six guardian deities. In addition, Fushou Palace has three subsidiary halls – the {{ill|Linshui Furen Hall|de|Linshui Furen Hall|ja|臨水夫人殿|zh|临水夫人殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Linshui Furen Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the {{ill|Huaguang Hall|de|Huaguang Hall|ja|华光殿|zh|华光殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Huaguang Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the {{ill|Jingzhu|de|Jingzhu|ja|境主|zh|境主|lt=Dawang|lt_ja=大王|lt_de=Dawang|lt_zh=大王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingzhu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Hall – which enshrine {{ill|Linshui Furen|de|Linshui Furen|ja|臨水夫人|zh|臨水夫人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Linshui Furen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Huaguang Dadi|de|Huaguang Dadi|ja|华光大帝|zh|华光大帝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Huaguang Dadi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the two kings, Huang and Zhao, respectively. == Deity System == The deities enshrined within Fushou Palace comprise figures from Manichaeism, [[:en:Taoism|Taoism]], [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], and local [[:en:Chinese folk religion|Folk Religion]], all merged within one palace. It is also the only Manichaean temple with a complete configuration and continuously maintained incense traditions to this day. === Main Deities === The principal deities of Fushou Palace are Mingjiao Wenfó and Dushi Zhenren (also known as Dushi Zhenjun), representing respectively the primary deity of Manichaeism, [[:en:Mani|Mani]] (216–270 AD), and the Xiapu Manichaean leader {{ill|Lin Deng|de|Lin Deng|ja|林瞪|zh|林瞪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lin Deng|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1003–1059 AD). {{multiple image | align = right | total_width = 260 | image1 = 浦西境福寿宫中的明教文佛.jpg | width1 = 300 | height1 = 350 | caption1 = Mingjiao Wenfó | image2 = 浦西境福寿宫中的度师真君.jpg | width2 = 300 | height2 = 350 | caption2 = Dushi Zhenjun | caption_align = center }} In the earliest studies, scholars such as {{ill|Li Linzhou|de|Li Linzhou|ja|李林洲|zh|李林洲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Li Linzhou|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} conducted field research and, equating Mingjiao with Manichaeism – citing the presence of “Wenfó” couplets in the modest lodgings of Manichaean temples – argued that the Mingjiao Wenfó in Fushou Palace was in fact the chief deity Mani Buddha of Manichaeism, with Dushi Zhenjun representing a certain Manichaean master. However, due to insufficient evidence, this view was challenged in 2004 by Manichaean scholars such as {{ill|Lin Wushu|de|Lin Wushu|ja|林悟殊|zh|林悟殊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lin Wushu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Between 2008 and 2009, with the discovery of the {{ill|Xiapu Documents|de|Xiapu Dokumente|ja|霞浦文書|zh|霞浦文书|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xiapu Documents|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a clearer understanding of the two main deities of the Mingjiao Wenfó Patriarch Hall emerged{{Cite news|title=Fuzhou Fushou Palace Has a Close Relationship with Mingjiao|author=Wen Hailong|work=Fuzhou Daily|date=2009-11-17|language=zh}}. The patterns and adornments on Mingjiao Wenfó, as well as many scenes depicted in the murals behind him – notably the sunburst on his head – resemble those found in Xiapu Manichaean artworks (a decorative element absent in other Taoist temples in Fuzhou), thereby confirming his affiliation with the Xiapu Manichaean system. At present, academic opinions on the identity of Mingjiao Wenfó vary; aside from the prevailing view that he is the Manichaean chief deity Mani, some scholars suggest that he may be a “Wenfó envoy” or related to what the Xiapu documents refer to as {{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata Wenfó|de|Shakyamuni Tathāgata Wenfó|ja|釈迦文佛|zh|釋迦文佛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata Wenfó|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Sizhou Great Saint|de|Sizhou Großer Heiliger|ja|泗州大聖|zh|泗州大聖|lt=Sizhou Wenfó|lt_ja=泗州文佛|lt_de=Sizhou Wenfó|lt_zh=泗州文佛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sizhou Great Saint|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Cite journal |author=Yang Fuxue |author2=Li Xiaoyan |author3=Peng Xiaojing |title=Major Findings from the Survey of Manichaean Relics in Fujian |journal=Studies in Religious Research |publication-place=[[:en:Chengdu|Chengdu]] |publication-date=2017 |issue=04 |page=259-271 |issn=1006-1312}} === Ancillary Deities in the Main Hall === {{Multiple image | image1 = 浦西福寿宫张圣真君.jpg | caption1 = Zhang Zhenjun | image2 = 浦西福寿宫玄天上帝.jpg | caption2 = Xuántiān Shangdi | perrow = 2/2 | align = right | total_width = 300 }} The ancestral hall enshrines, on the west and east sides of its north wall (the side where the principal deity is located), the deities {{ill|Zhang Zhenjun|de|Zhang Zhenjun|ja|張真君|zh|张圣真君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhang Zhenjun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Xuanwu (god)|Xuántiān Shangdi]], both figures associated with Taoism. {{NoteTag|Zhang Zhenjun (also known as Zhang Zhenjun or Zhang Shengjun) is the chief of the Thunder Regulation of the Thirty-Three Heavens, born in {{ill|Yongtai County|de|Yongtai County|ja|永泰县|zh|永泰县|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yongtai County|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a direct disciple of {{ill|Xu Zhenjun|de|Xu Zhenjun|ja|許真君|zh|许真君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xu Zhenjun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Lushan Sect. Local tradition holds that Lin Deng once studied the Daoist techniques transmitted by Xu Zhenjun on Lushan. Additionally, the Xiapu Documents mention information related to the Lushan Sect. Another reason for enshrining Zhang Zhenjun may be that Lin Deng was conferred the title “Dongtian Dulei Shi” (as rendered in various sources) in recognition of his merit in praying for rain.}} According to local tradition, Zhang Zhenjun is the Thunder Regulation Chief; indeed, Fuzhou still preserves the {{ill|Zhang Zhenjun Ancestral Hall|de|Zhang Zhenjun Ancestral Hall|ja|張聖真君祖殿|zh|张圣真君祖殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhang Zhenjun Ancestral Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Minqing County|de|Minqing County|ja|闽清县|zh|闽清县|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minqing County|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Cite web|date=2023-06-27|title=Physical Geography|url=http://www.fzmq.gov.cn/xjwz/zjmq/zrdl/201704/t20170413_391146.htm|access-date=2023-10-30|website=Minqing County Government|archive-date=2022-11-20|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221120015517/http://www.fzmq.gov.cn/xjwz/zjmq/zrdl/201704/t20170413_391146.htm|dead-url=no}} and the Fujian Provincial Cultural Heritage Unit {{ill|Zhang Zhenjun Ancestral Hall|de|Zhang Zhenjun Ancestral Hall|ja|張真君祖殿|zh|张真君祖殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhang Zhenjun Ancestral Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (located in [[:en:Taijiang, Fuzhou|Taijiang District]]). Xuántiān Shangdi, as recorded in the Xiapu Documents, is referred to as “Northern Guardian Zhenwu Bodhisattva”{{NoteTag|Term derived from the title conferred by Emperor Yongle: “Northern Polar Guardian Zhenwu Xuántiān Shangdi”}} or “Great Saint Zhenwu”. He is a Taoist figure of the {{ill|Xuanwu Sect|de|Xuanwu Sect|ja|玄武派|zh|玄武派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xuanwu Sect|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, regarded as the 82nd transformation of the Supreme Elderly Lord and one of Taoism’s four major guardian deities (the others being the Azure Dragon, White Tiger, and Vermilion Bird). In most Taoist temples, figures from the Lushan and Xuanwu sects do not coexist; however, Fushou Palace enshrines deities from both, reflecting its unique position as a Manichaean temple that both draws on and distinguishes itself from Taoism. === Three Halls and One Pavilion === Fushou Palace features four subsidiary halls: on the left is the {{ill|Linshui Furen Hall|de|Linshui Furen Hall|ja|臨水夫人殿|zh|临水夫人殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Linshui Furen Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while on the right are the {{ill|Guanyin Pavilion|de|Guanyin Pavillon|ja|観音閣|zh|观音阁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guanyin Pavilion|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the hall of {{ill|Huaguang Dadi|de|Huaguang Dadi|ja|华光大帝|zh|华光大帝|lt=Huaguang|lt_ja=华光|lt_de=Huaguang|lt_zh=华光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Huaguang Dadi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the hall of {{ill|Jingzhu|de|Jingzhu|ja|境主|zh|境主|lt=Dawang|lt_ja=大王|lt_de=Dawang|lt_zh=Dawang|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingzhu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. According to inscriptions on old stone tablets preserved within the palace, adjacent to the original site of Fushou Palace were the Yulin Palace (dedicated to Linshui Furen) and Dawang Hall, while the Guanyin Pavilion and Huaguang Hall were later additions. The deities in these halls belong respectively to the [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] and Taoist systems. == Notes == {{NoteFoot}} == See Also == {{Commons category}} {{fzcuo|1742|Puxi Fushou Temple}} * {{ill|Leshantang|de|Leshantang|ja|乐山堂|zh|乐山堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Leshantang|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Tahou Third Buddha Pagoda|de|Tahou Third Buddha Pagoda|ja|塔后三佛塔|zh|塔后三佛塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahou Third Buddha Pagoda|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Feilu Pagoda|de|Feilu Pagoda|ja|飞路塔|zh|飞路塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feilu Pagoda|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Cao'an|de|Cao'an|ja|草庵|zh|草庵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cao'an|1=sōan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24897528}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Manichaeism footer}} {{Manichaeism}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:浦西境福寿宫]] [[zh:浦西境福寿宫]] [[ja:浦西福寿宮]] [[de:Puxi Fushou Temple]] [[cdo:浦西福壽宮]] {{Commons category|Puxi Fushou Temple}} {{translated page|zh|浦西境福寿宫|version=86359806|comment=Imported full zh history}} {{wikidata link|Q122897824}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:de import failed]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[cdo:Puō-să̤ Hók-sêu-gṳ̆ng]][[commons:Category:Puxi Fushou Temple]][[zh:浦西境福寿宫]] [[cdo:Puō-să̤ Hók-sêu-gṳ̆ng]][[commons:Category:Puxi Fushou Temple]][[zh:浦西境福寿宫]] [[cdo:Puō-să̤ Hók-sêu-gṳ̆ng]][[sn:commons:Category:Puxi Fushou Temple]][[zh:浦西境福寿宫]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: [[da:d:Q122897824]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:25, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | building_name = Puxi Fushou Temple
（Mingjiao Wenfó Patriarch Hall） | image = 浦西福寿宫外景 03.jpg | caption = Exterior view of Puxi Fushou Temple | coordinates = {{coord|26|3|46.76|N|119|17|23.82|E|scale:10000|display=title,inline}} | mapframe-zoom = 13 | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Manichaeism|Manichaeism]]（[[:en:Manichaeism|Manichaeism]]）
[[:en:Taoism|Taoism]]、[[:en:Chinese folk religion|Fujian Folk Religion]] | heritage = [[:en:Taijiang, Fuzhou|Taijiang District]] Immovable Cultural Relic | architecture_type = [[:en:Temple|Temple]] | totalarea = Approximately 900㎡ | location = {{PRC}}[[:en:Fujian|Fujian Province]][[:en:Fuzhou|Fuzhou]][[:en:Taijiang, Fuzhou|Taijiang District]]{{ill|Yizhou Subdistrict|de|Yizhou Stadtteil|ja|義洲街道|zh|义洲街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yizhou Subdistrict|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Puxi Village 6-1 }} '''Fushou Palace'''{{Cite journal |author=Lin Wushu |title=An Analysis of the Religious Attributes of “Mingjiao Wenfó” at Puxi Fushou Temple in Fuzhou |journal=Journal of Sun Yat-sen University (Social Sciences Edition) |publication-date=2004 |issue=06 |page=118-123 |issn=1000-9639}}, also known as '''Puxi Region Fushou Palace'''{{Cite journal |author=Peng Xiaojing |author2=Yang Fuxue |title=An Investigation into the Manichaean Deities at Fushou Palace in Fuzhou |journal=World Religious Studies |publication-date=2016 |issue=03 |page=118-128 |issn=1000-4289}} and '''Fuzhou Fushou Palace'''{{Cite journal |author=Lin Zizhou |title=A Comparative Study of Manichaean Relics between Xiapu and Fushou Palace in Fuzhou |journal={{ill|Shihezi University|de|Shihezi University|ja|石河子大学|zh|石河子大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shihezi University|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Journal (Philosophy and Social Sciences Edition) |publication-place=[[:en:Shihezi|Shihezi City]] |publication-date=2016 |issue=06 |page=33-39 |doi=10.13880/j.cnki.cn65-1210/c.20161221.010 |issn=1671-0304}}, formerly known as '''Mingjiao Wenfó Patriarch Hall''', is a religious building located in the Puxi community of [[:en:Fuzhou|Fuzhou]], [[:en:Taijiang, Fuzhou|Taijiang District]], {{ill|Yizhou Subdistrict|de|Yizhou Stadtteil|ja|義洲街道|zh|义洲街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yizhou Subdistrict|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Fujian|Fujian Province]]. It is the only remaining [[:en:Manichaeism|Manichaean]] building in Fuzhou and is known as a “living fossil of Manichaean culture”. It is currently an immovable cultural relic site in Taijiang District, Fuzhou.{{Cite news|url=http://kejiao.cntv.cn/20120509/100101.shtml|title=Puxi Fushou Temple in Taijiang Confirmed as a Manichaean Temple Ruin|work={{ill|China Network Television|de|China Network Television|ja|中国民間テレビ局|zh|中国网络电视台|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China Network Television|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|date=2012-05-09|accessdate=2023-10-01|language=zh|archive-date=2021-10-21|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211021213535/http://kejiao.cntv.cn/20120509/100101.shtml|dead-url=no}} Puxi Fushou Temple is also one of the thirteen halls in the faith of {{ill|Jiu'an Taishan|de|Jiu'an Taishan|ja|九案泰山|zh|九案泰山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jiu'an Taishan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == History == [[File:浦西福寿宫内殿.jpg|thumb|Interior Hall of Puxi Fushou Temple]] The Mingjiao Wenfó Patriarch Hall was originally built during the Song Dynasty and has undergone multiple restorations and relocations over the years. According to the records in the [[:en:Wanli Emperor|Wanli]] edition of the {{ill|Funing Prefecture|de|Funing Präfektur|ja|福寧州|zh|福宁州|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funing Prefecture|1=Funing Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16078627}} Gazetteer, the hall was constructed to commemorate the Manichaean patriarch {{ill|Lin Deng|de|Lin Deng|ja|林瞪|zh|林瞪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lin Deng|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who prayed for rain in {{ill|Min County|de|Min Landkreis|ja|閩縣|zh|闽县|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Min County|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} during the {{ill|Jiayou|de|Jiayou|ja|嘉祐|zh|嘉祐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jiayou|1=Jiayou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10924055}} era of the Northern Song. Due to the generally prohibitive attitude toward Mingjiao during Chinese history (especially in the {{ill|Ming Dynasty|de|Ming-Dynasty|ja|明朝|zh|明朝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ming Dynasty|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明|1=Ming dynasty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9903}}), local believers of the Xiao clan outwardly converted to [[:en:Taoism|Taoism]], enshrining other Taoist and folk deities to secretly safeguard the deity of Mingjiao Wenfó{{Cite journal |author=Li Linzhou |title=An Important Manichaean Site in Fuzhou — Fushou Palace in Yizhou, Taijiang, Fuzhou |journal=Fujian Religion |publication-date=2004 |issue=01 |page=43-44}}. Until 1997, the original site of the ancestral hall was located at the present-day Fuzhou MSG Factory; in 1997, due to the construction of the factory on that site, it was relocated to its current location in Puxi{{Cite journal |author=Chen Yizhou |author2=Tu Yuanji |title=A Study on the Manichaean Temple Ruins in Fujian |journal=Maritime History Research |publication-place=[[:en:Quanzhou|Quanzhou]] |publication-date=2004 |issue=01 |page=75-83 |issn=1006-8384}}. Owing to the unclear religious affiliation at the time, in 1998 the authorities renamed Wenfó Patriarch Hall as “Fushou Palace” to grant it a legitimate religious status and incorporated it into Fuzhou’s [[:en:Taoism|Taoism]] temple system{{Cite book|title=Fuzhou Annals (1995—2005)|last=Fuzhou Local Annals Compilation Committee|publisher=Fangzhi Press|year=2017|isbn=978-7-5034-9649-3|location=Beijing|pages=826}}; in the early 2000s, due to insufficient evidence, there remained considerable academic debate over its religious affiliation, until after the discovery and study of the {{ill|Xiapu Documents|de|Xiapu Dokumente|ja|霞浦文書|zh|霞浦文书|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xiapu Documents|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 2008 confirmed that Fushou Palace is indeed a [[:en:Manichaeism|Manichaeism]] ({{ill|Mingjiao|de|Mingjiao|ja|明教|zh|明教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mingjiao|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 唐代三夷教|1=Three Persian religions in Tang dynasty|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418516}}) temple – the best-preserved and most fully equipped of its kind today. The Mingjiao Wenfó Patriarch Hall also has a subsidiary hall located in Shisha Natural Village, {{ill|Shangjie Town|de|Shangjie Town|ja|上街鎮|zh|上街镇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shangjie Town|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Minhou County. This temple originated over 200 years ago when local believers’ ancestors branched off from the ancestral hall; the two main deities enshrined here are identical to those of the main hall. == Architecture == The original site of the Mingjiao Wenfó Patriarch Hall (up to 1997) faced east with its entrance oriented westward, in line with the Manichaean concept of venerating the west. It covered an area of 3.5 mu with a building area of 2 mu, comprising a main hall, an auxiliary hall, a performance stage, a banquet viewing platform, a kitchen, and a garden. The current site of Puxi Fushou Temple spans over 900 square meters and faces south. The main building occupies 742.6 square meters, has a depth of 22.6 meters, a main hall width of 14.7 meters, and a height of 10 meters. It primarily enshrines two principal deities: the Mingjiao Wenfó modeled after [[:en:Mani|Mani]], and Dushi Zhenjun modeled after the Fujian Manichaean patriarch {{ill|Lin Deng|de|Lin Deng|ja|林瞪|zh|林瞪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lin Deng|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the main hall, flanking the principal deity on its left and right are the auxiliary deities {{ill|Zhang Zhenjun|de|Zhang Zhenjun|ja|張真君|zh|张真君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhang Zhenjun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Xuanwu (god)|de|Zhenwu Dadi|ja|真武大帝|zh|真武大帝|lt=Zhenwu Dadi|Xuanwu (god)=Xuanwu (god)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xuanwu (god)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, respectively, along with thirty-six guardian deities. In addition, Fushou Palace has three subsidiary halls – the {{ill|Linshui Furen Hall|de|Linshui Furen Hall|ja|臨水夫人殿|zh|临水夫人殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Linshui Furen Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the {{ill|Huaguang Hall|de|Huaguang Hall|ja|华光殿|zh|华光殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Huaguang Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the {{ill|Jingzhu|de|Jingzhu|ja|境主|zh|境主|lt=Dawang|lt_ja=大王|lt_de=Dawang|lt_zh=大王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingzhu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Hall – which enshrine {{ill|Linshui Furen|de|Linshui Furen|ja|臨水夫人|zh|臨水夫人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Linshui Furen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Huaguang Dadi|de|Huaguang Dadi|ja|华光大帝|zh|华光大帝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Huaguang Dadi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the two kings, Huang and Zhao, respectively. == Deity System == The deities enshrined within Fushou Palace comprise figures from Manichaeism, [[:en:Taoism|Taoism]], [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]], and local [[:en:Chinese folk religion|Folk Religion]], all merged within one palace. It is also the only Manichaean temple with a complete configuration and continuously maintained incense traditions to this day. === Main Deities === The principal deities of Fushou Palace are Mingjiao Wenfó and Dushi Zhenren (also known as Dushi Zhenjun), representing respectively the primary deity of Manichaeism, [[:en:Mani|Mani]] (216–270 AD), and the Xiapu Manichaean leader {{ill|Lin Deng|de|Lin Deng|ja|林瞪|zh|林瞪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lin Deng|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (1003–1059 AD). {{multiple image | align = right | total_width = 260 | image1 = 浦西境福寿宫中的明教文佛.jpg | width1 = 300 | height1 = 350 | caption1 = Mingjiao Wenfó | image2 = 浦西境福寿宫中的度师真君.jpg | width2 = 300 | height2 = 350 | caption2 = Dushi Zhenjun | caption_align = center }} In the earliest studies, scholars such as {{ill|Li Linzhou|de|Li Linzhou|ja|李林洲|zh|李林洲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Li Linzhou|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} conducted field research and, equating Mingjiao with Manichaeism – citing the presence of “Wenfó” couplets in the modest lodgings of Manichaean temples – argued that the Mingjiao Wenfó in Fushou Palace was in fact the chief deity Mani Buddha of Manichaeism, with Dushi Zhenjun representing a certain Manichaean master. However, due to insufficient evidence, this view was challenged in 2004 by Manichaean scholars such as {{ill|Lin Wushu|de|Lin Wushu|ja|林悟殊|zh|林悟殊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lin Wushu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Between 2008 and 2009, with the discovery of the {{ill|Xiapu Documents|de|Xiapu Dokumente|ja|霞浦文書|zh|霞浦文书|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xiapu Documents|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a clearer understanding of the two main deities of the Mingjiao Wenfó Patriarch Hall emerged{{Cite news|title=Fuzhou Fushou Palace Has a Close Relationship with Mingjiao|author=Wen Hailong|work=Fuzhou Daily|date=2009-11-17|language=zh}}. The patterns and adornments on Mingjiao Wenfó, as well as many scenes depicted in the murals behind him – notably the sunburst on his head – resemble those found in Xiapu Manichaean artworks (a decorative element absent in other Taoist temples in Fuzhou), thereby confirming his affiliation with the Xiapu Manichaean system. At present, academic opinions on the identity of Mingjiao Wenfó vary; aside from the prevailing view that he is the Manichaean chief deity Mani, some scholars suggest that he may be a “Wenfó envoy” or related to what the Xiapu documents refer to as {{ill|Shakyamuni Tathāgata Wenfó|de|Shakyamuni Tathāgata Wenfó|ja|釈迦文佛|zh|釋迦文佛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shakyamuni Tathāgata Wenfó|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Sizhou Great Saint|de|Sizhou Großer Heiliger|ja|泗州大聖|zh|泗州大聖|lt=Sizhou Wenfó|lt_ja=泗州文佛|lt_de=Sizhou Wenfó|lt_zh=泗州文佛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sizhou Great Saint|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Cite journal |author=Yang Fuxue |author2=Li Xiaoyan |author3=Peng Xiaojing |title=Major Findings from the Survey of Manichaean Relics in Fujian |journal=Studies in Religious Research |publication-place=[[:en:Chengdu|Chengdu]] |publication-date=2017 |issue=04 |page=259-271 |issn=1006-1312}} === Ancillary Deities in the Main Hall === {{Multiple image | image1 = 浦西福寿宫张圣真君.jpg | caption1 = Zhang Zhenjun | image2 = 浦西福寿宫玄天上帝.jpg | caption2 = Xuántiān Shangdi | perrow = 2/2 | align = right | total_width = 300 }} The ancestral hall enshrines, on the west and east sides of its north wall (the side where the principal deity is located), the deities {{ill|Zhang Zhenjun|de|Zhang Zhenjun|ja|張真君|zh|张圣真君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhang Zhenjun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and [[:en:Xuanwu (god)|Xuántiān Shangdi]], both figures associated with Taoism. {{NoteTag|Zhang Zhenjun (also known as Zhang Zhenjun or Zhang Shengjun) is the chief of the Thunder Regulation of the Thirty-Three Heavens, born in {{ill|Yongtai County|de|Yongtai County|ja|永泰县|zh|永泰县|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yongtai County|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and a direct disciple of {{ill|Xu Zhenjun|de|Xu Zhenjun|ja|許真君|zh|许真君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xu Zhenjun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Lushan Sect. Local tradition holds that Lin Deng once studied the Daoist techniques transmitted by Xu Zhenjun on Lushan. Additionally, the Xiapu Documents mention information related to the Lushan Sect. Another reason for enshrining Zhang Zhenjun may be that Lin Deng was conferred the title “Dongtian Dulei Shi” (as rendered in various sources) in recognition of his merit in praying for rain.}} According to local tradition, Zhang Zhenjun is the Thunder Regulation Chief; indeed, Fuzhou still preserves the {{ill|Zhang Zhenjun Ancestral Hall|de|Zhang Zhenjun Ancestral Hall|ja|張聖真君祖殿|zh|张圣真君祖殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhang Zhenjun Ancestral Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Minqing County|de|Minqing County|ja|闽清县|zh|闽清县|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minqing County|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Cite web|date=2023-06-27|title=Physical Geography|url=http://www.fzmq.gov.cn/xjwz/zjmq/zrdl/201704/t20170413_391146.htm|access-date=2023-10-30|website=Minqing County Government|archive-date=2022-11-20|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221120015517/http://www.fzmq.gov.cn/xjwz/zjmq/zrdl/201704/t20170413_391146.htm|dead-url=no}} and the Fujian Provincial Cultural Heritage Unit {{ill|Zhang Zhenjun Ancestral Hall|de|Zhang Zhenjun Ancestral Hall|ja|張真君祖殿|zh|张真君祖殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhang Zhenjun Ancestral Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (located in [[:en:Taijiang, Fuzhou|Taijiang District]]). Xuántiān Shangdi, as recorded in the Xiapu Documents, is referred to as “Northern Guardian Zhenwu Bodhisattva”{{NoteTag|Term derived from the title conferred by Emperor Yongle: “Northern Polar Guardian Zhenwu Xuántiān Shangdi”}} or “Great Saint Zhenwu”. He is a Taoist figure of the {{ill|Xuanwu Sect|de|Xuanwu Sect|ja|玄武派|zh|玄武派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Xuanwu Sect|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, regarded as the 82nd transformation of the Supreme Elderly Lord and one of Taoism’s four major guardian deities (the others being the Azure Dragon, White Tiger, and Vermilion Bird). In most Taoist temples, figures from the Lushan and Xuanwu sects do not coexist; however, Fushou Palace enshrines deities from both, reflecting its unique position as a Manichaean temple that both draws on and distinguishes itself from Taoism. === Three Halls and One Pavilion === Fushou Palace features four subsidiary halls: on the left is the {{ill|Linshui Furen Hall|de|Linshui Furen Hall|ja|臨水夫人殿|zh|临水夫人殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Linshui Furen Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while on the right are the {{ill|Guanyin Pavilion|de|Guanyin Pavillon|ja|観音閣|zh|观音阁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guanyin Pavilion|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the hall of {{ill|Huaguang Dadi|de|Huaguang Dadi|ja|华光大帝|zh|华光大帝|lt=Huaguang|lt_ja=华光|lt_de=Huaguang|lt_zh=华光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Huaguang Dadi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the hall of {{ill|Jingzhu|de|Jingzhu|ja|境主|zh|境主|lt=Dawang|lt_ja=大王|lt_de=Dawang|lt_zh=Dawang|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingzhu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. According to inscriptions on old stone tablets preserved within the palace, adjacent to the original site of Fushou Palace were the Yulin Palace (dedicated to Linshui Furen) and Dawang Hall, while the Guanyin Pavilion and Huaguang Hall were later additions. The deities in these halls belong respectively to the [[:en:Buddhism|Buddhism]] and Taoist systems. == Notes == {{NoteFoot}} == See Also == {{Commons category}} {{fzcuo|1742|Puxi Fushou Temple}} * {{ill|Leshantang|de|Leshantang|ja|乐山堂|zh|乐山堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Leshantang|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Tahou Third Buddha Pagoda|de|Tahou Third Buddha Pagoda|ja|塔后三佛塔|zh|塔后三佛塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tahou Third Buddha Pagoda|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Feilu Pagoda|de|Feilu Pagoda|ja|飞路塔|zh|飞路塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Feilu Pagoda|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Cao'an|de|Cao'an|ja|草庵|zh|草庵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cao'an|1=sōan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24897528}} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Manichaeism footer}} {{Manichaeism}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:浦西境福寿宫]] [[zh:浦西境福寿宫]] [[ja:浦西福寿宮]] [[de:Puxi Fushou Temple]] [[cdo:浦西福壽宮]] {{Commons category|Puxi Fushou Temple}} {{translated page|zh|浦西境福寿宫|version=86359806|comment=Imported full zh history}} {{wikidata link|Q122897824}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:de import failed]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
